En: One more thing before we do... He glances at the booth again. "We don't have to talk to them immediately. We can walk right past them, continue with our business."
Zh: “还有一件事……”他又瞥了一眼包厢。“我们不用马上跟他们谈。可以先直接走过去，继续我们的工作。”

En: Good. A power move. *Purposefully* concentrate on something else first.
Zh: 很好。这一步走得很漂亮。*有目的地*集中在其他事情上。

En: But... aren't you *curious* to know what they have to say about the murder?
Zh: 但是……难道你不*好奇*他们会怎么说这次凶杀案吗？

En: They're in no hurry to leave -- they think they own the place... Anyway, I leave that choice to you. Whatever you decide is fine by me.
Zh: “他们不会着急离开——他们觉得这里是他们的地盘……话说回来，选择权就交给你了。不管你做出什么决定，我都没意见。”

En: Yeah -- streetwise. Zoom right past, do it on your own terms.
Zh: 是啊——这就是街头智慧。直接走过去，按你自己的方式去做。

En: And one more thing... He pulls the black plastic over the dead man's face, then looks at you. "This was *very* good work, detective."
Zh: “还有一件事……”他拉开死者脸上的黑色塑料，然后看向你。“你做的*非常棒*，警探。”

En: Oh well -- we'll get the armoured boots in another lifetime.
Zh: 好吧——我们下辈子再弄双装甲靴穿吧。

En: I'm just glad he's gone. We have other matters to attend to.
Zh: “他走了我很高兴。我们还有其他的事要做。”

En: Well, he's in processing now. We have other matters to attend to.
Zh: “呃，他已经交到分析部了。我们还有其他的事要做。”

En: Oh well -- we'll get the armoured boots in another lifetime.
Zh: 好吧——我们下辈子再弄双装甲靴穿吧。

En: He won't even *acknowledge* the stolen boots you're wearing. For him, they don't exist. You'll have to bring it up yourself, later -- *if* you dare.
Zh: 他甚至都不会*知道*你穿的是一双偷来的鞋子。对于他来说，它们根本不存在。之后你得自己提出来——*如果*你有这个胆量的话。

En: Looks like we can get to work at once. The Union muscle turned up. He points to the mess hall doors. "They look rowdy. We should talk to them."
Zh: “看来我们马上就要开始工作了。工会的人来了。”他指着餐厅的门。“他们看起来很爱惹是生非。我们应该跟他们聊聊。”

En: Let's roll. [Leave.]
Zh: “我们走吧。”[离开。]

En: What do you mean 'rowdy'?
Zh: “你说*爱惹是生非*是什么意思？”

En: Why do we need to talk to them?
Zh: “为什么要跟他们谈？”

En: I mean ungovernable. Martinaise isn't exactly enthusiastic about the RCM being here. They prefer to be *policed* by the Union -- these men here...
Zh: “我的意思是很难管制。马丁内斯对RCM可不是很热情。他们更喜欢被工会*管制*——就是这里那些人……”

En: There are so many of them. Maybe we should call in reinforcements?
Zh: “他们人好多。也许我们应该呼叫增援？”

En: That would just escalate tensions. No captain would sign off on it. Solving one murder isn't worth a conflict between the RCM and the Débardeurs' Union.
Zh: “那样只会让紧张局势加剧。没有警长愿意签字负责的。解决一起凶杀案而已，不值得引起RCM和装卸工会的冲突。”

En: In fact, even the *death* of two detectives might not warrant an all-out war. So let's keep a cool head, okay?
Zh: “事实上，甚至两个警探的*死*也许都不会引爆全面战争。所以一定要保持头脑冷静，好吗？”

En: Looks like we can get to work at once. The Union muscle turned up. He points to the mess hall doors. "They look rowdy. We should talk to them."
Zh: “看来我们马上就要开始工作了。工会的人来了——”他指着餐厅的门。“他们看起来很爱惹是生非。我们应该跟他们聊聊。”

En: Good, thanks.
Zh: “好的，谢谢。”

En: I don't know if I was ready to let him go...
Zh: “我不确定自己有没有准备好放他走……”

En: Well, he's in processing now. We have other matters to attend to.
Zh: “呃，现在已经有人在处理他的事了。我们还有其他的事要做。”

En: I'm just glad he's gone. We have other matters to attend to.
Zh: “他走了我很高兴。我们还有其他的事要做。”

En: The lieutenant is downplaying his contribution -- he got up before sunrise to get this done. And now he's quite pleased with himself for taking care of the gruesome business so efficiently.
Zh: 警督把自己的贡献轻描淡写地带过了——今天太阳还没出来之前，他就已经起来把这件事处理好了。现在他很高兴自己能这么有效率地处理掉这种令人厌恶的工作。

En: He kind of wishes you'd acknowledged his contribution, but you've missed your opportunity now.
Zh: 他有点希望你能承认他的贡献，但是现在你已经失去这个机会了。

En: He points to the mess hall doors. "The Union muscle finally turned up, and they look rowdy. We need to talk to them."
Zh: 他指着餐厅大门。“工会的人总算出现了，他们看起来很爱惹是生非，我们得跟他们谈谈。”

En: I've got some good news. The lieutenant smiles. "I took care of the body. The thought of him decomposing in my MC wouldn't let me sleep."
Zh: “我有些好消息。”警督笑了。“那具尸体我处理好了。一想到他还在我的MC腐烂，我连觉都睡不好了。”

En: One loose thread less to worry about -- and one big problem to replace it. He crosses something out.
Zh: 他划掉了一些东西。“少了一点小烦恼——换成了一个大麻烦。”

En: Everything points to the Dockworkers' Union: the belt used for hanging him, tracks in the mud, the circumstances in Martinaise, my preliminary information...
Zh: “一切都指向了码头工人工会：用来上吊的皮带，泥地里的痕迹，马丁内斯的环境，我得到的事前资料……”

En: Everything points to the Dockworkers' Union: the *belt* used for hanging him, the circumstances in Martinaise, my preliminary information...
Zh: “一切都指向了装卸工会：那根把他吊起来的*皮带*，马丁内斯的环境，我手上的事前资料……”

En: Everything points to the Dockworkers' Union: the tracks in the mud, the circumstances in Martinaise, my preliminary information...
Zh: “一切都指向了装卸工会：泥地里的痕迹，马丁内斯的环境，我手上的事前资料……”

En: We can't be sure of course -- Evrart may have lied to us. He definitely has an agenda of his own. Either way, we need to interview them. And it won't be easy.
Zh: “当然了，我们也无法确定——艾弗拉特也许对我们撒谎了。他肯定有自己的议程。不管怎么说，我们得走访他们。而且这绝对不是什么简单的工作。”

En: Which may, of course, all be wrong. But we still need to talk to them -- and it won't be easy.
Zh: “当然了，也有可能全都是错的。不过我们还是得走访他们——这绝对不是什么简单的工作。”

En: A lot of things point to the Dockworkers' Union: the circumstances in Martinaise, my preliminary information. Hmm, actually...
Zh: “很多事情都指向了装卸工会：马丁内斯的环境，我手上的事前资料。嗯，其实……”

En: ...that's not *a lot* of things. We should have dug up more yesterday. Still, we need to talk to them. And it won't be easy.
Zh: “……好像也*不算*很多事情。昨天我们应该多挖点消息的。不管怎么说，我们还是得走访他们——这绝对不是什么简单的工作。”

En: Men who drink beer for breakfast. I have no doubt these are the Hardie boys Evrart told us about, the ones who *keep the peace* around here.
Zh: “那些把啤酒当早餐的人。毫无疑问，他们就是艾弗拉特口中的哈迪兄弟，那些在这里*维持秩序*的人。”

En: Men who drink beer for breakfast? There's talk of an armed wing of the Union called *the Hardie boys*, who are responsible for said *policing*. I think it's them.
Zh: “那些把啤酒当早餐的人？大家说工会有一支叫*哈迪兄弟*的武装小队，据说是专门负责*维持治安*的。我觉得应该就是他们。”

En: And the big one, he squints his eyes, studying the men in the mess hall, "must be Hardie himself..."
Zh: “还有那个大个子，”他眯起眼睛，研究着餐厅里的那个男人，“一定就是哈迪本人……”

En: Are these the men Garte told us about yesterday?
Zh: “他们就是昨天加尔特说的那群人吗？”

En: I completely forgot. He looks at his notes. "Sorry, I had a rough night's sleep. It's them, by the looks of it -- loud and nasty, just like the manager said."
Zh: “我完全忘记了。”他看着自己的笔记。“抱歉，我昨晚睡的不好。从外表来看，是他们没错——又吵又粗鲁，跟经理口中的一样。”

En: He's not exaggerating about that mortal danger. Just calmly factoring it in. Your fists clench and your pulse rises uncomfortably.
Zh: 他说的有生命危险，一点也不夸张。只要冷静地把这一点纳入考虑范围就好。你的拳头紧握，脉搏也升高到让人不适的程度。

En: This is clearly the Union's *peacekeeping* force Evrart told us about. The one he said hanged our victim.
Zh: “他们肯定就是艾弗拉特说过的——工会里*维护和平*的力量，吊死被害人的那群人。”

En: He's avoiding your gaze. This is the time to say how sorry you are about how things went.
Zh: 他避开了你的视线。这种时候应该说你为发生的事情感到很抱歉。

En: Can't bring the dead back.
Zh: 人死不能复生。

En: They have to. It's not your place to live their lives.
Zh: 他们必须这样。这是他们的生活，不是你的。

En: They have to. It's not your place to live their lives.
Zh: 他们必须这样。这是他们的生活，不是你的。

En: I really have, he thinks. She was strong. She made it easy for us.
Zh: 我真有过，他想到。她很坚强。她让我们轻松了不少。

En: That did not go...
Zh: “不是很……”

En: *Well*? No, it didn't.
Zh: “*顺利*？不，是不顺利。”

En: You look south, over the crumbling balustrade. There -- 1.2 kilometres away -- a piece of broken stone cuts into the sky. A tomb. On it, a mural that used to say: tomorrow is just a whisper away. You cannot *really* see the Feld building from here, but somehow you feel like you should return to it.
Zh: 你看向南边，越过摇摇欲坠的栏杆。在那里——1.2公里开外的地方——一块破碎的石头直插云霄。那是一座坟墓。在那上面，曾经有一幅壁画：明日近在眼前。从这里你并不能*真正*看见菲尔德大厦，不过不知道为什么，你觉得自己应该回去看看。

En: So, the death notification... the lieutenant says as soon as you've left the apartment.
Zh: “所以，死亡通知的事……”你一离开公寓，警督就开了口。

En: I'm sorry, I know I fucked up.
Zh: “抱歉，我知道我搞砸了。”

En: There's nothing to discuss, let's move on. [Leave.]
Zh: “没什么要讨论的，我们走吧。”[离开。]

En: Well, I'll call the station when we're done with the day and inform them about the identity of the deceased.
Zh: “呃，等今天的工作结束之后，我给警局去个电话，通知他们死者的身份。”

En: You did well, the lieutenant says as soon as you've left the apartment. The balcony feels cool and quiet, with a stunning view over the sleeping district.
Zh: “你做的很好，”一离开公寓，警督就开了口。阳台上凉爽又清静，能够很好地看见整片沉睡的区域。

En: (Nod.) "What now?"
Zh: （点头。）“那现在呢？”

En: Great, the case is closed then. [Leave.]
Zh: “很好，那这个案子就结束了。”[离开。]

En: I'll call the station when we're finished with the day and let them know the name of the deceased.
Zh: “等今天的工作结束之后，我给警局去个电话，让他们知道死者的名字。”

En: Sure, I'll call the station when we're done with the day. Let's go.
Zh: “当然。等今天的工作结束之后，我给警局去个电话。我们走吧。”

En: It's alright, don't worry about it. His eyes wander over the sleeping district.
Zh: “没事的，别担心。”他的眼睛扫视着这个沉睡的区域。

En: I could have done more.
Zh: “我本来可以做更多事情的。”

En: You did enough. He pauses, rubbing his hands together to generate some heat.
Zh: “你做的够多了。”他停顿了一下，摩擦着双手想要取暖。

En: I'll call the station when we're finished with the day and let them know the name of the deceased.
Zh: “等今天的工作结束之后，我给警局去个电话，让她们知道死者的名字。”

En: But yes, that's on me. Let's get on with the murder investigation.
Zh: “但是没错，那件事交给我。我们还是继续调查凶杀案吧。”

En: It's alright, don't worry about it. His eyes wander over the streets.
Zh: “没事的，别担心。”他的眼睛扫视着街道。

En: I'll call the station tomorrow and let them know the name of the deceased.
Zh: “明天我给警局去个电话，让她们知道死者的名字。”

En: That's it?
Zh: “就这样？”

En: What about Billie and her kids?
Zh: “那比莉和她的孩子呢？”

En: That's it. We should get back to our case. There's nothing more we can do here.
Zh: “就这样了。我们应该回到自己的案件上来。我们在这里已经没什么可以做的了。”

En: There's not much we can do for them anymore, I'm afraid.
Zh: “恐怕我们也没什么能为她们做的了。”

En: Right, lets try to get back to it then. [Leave.]
Zh: “没错，那就重新回到案子上去吧。”[离开。]

En: He looks at you. "And... officer -- I've seen worse. This wasn't the worst I've seen, okay? Now, let's go."
Zh: 他看着你。“还有……警官——我见过更糟糕的。这并不是我见过最糟糕的情况，好吗？现在，我们还是走吧。”

En: Can't save the whole world, you know?
Zh: “我们不可能拯救整个世界，你明白的。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: I'll call the station tomorrow and let them know the name of the deceased.
Zh: “明天我给警局去个电话，让她们知道死者的名字。”

En: What about Billie and her kids?
Zh: “那比莉和她的孩子呢？”

En: He nods. "Let's go."
Zh: 他点点头。“我们走吧。”

En: That's it?
Zh: “就这样？”

En: They'll manage.
Zh: “他们会处理的。”

En: Right, this is closed then. [Leave.]
Zh: “没错，那这个案子就结束了。”[离开。]

En: That's it. We should get back to our case now that our duty here is done.
Zh: “就这样了。既然我们在这里的职责已经完成，还是回到我们的案子上去吧。”

En: Anyway, I'll call the station when we're finished with the day and let them know the name of the deceased.
Zh: “不管怎么说，等今天的工作结束之后，我给警局去个电话，让她们知道死者的名字。”

En: Anyway, I'll call the station tomorrow and let them know the name of the deceased.
Zh: “不管怎么说，明天我给警局去个电话，让她们知道死者的名字。”

En: Alright, but -- and, forgive me, this is just something I have to ask -- if you have shoes, then *why aren't you wearing them*?
Zh: “好吧，不过 - 不好意思，请恕我冒昧的问一下 - 如果你有鞋的话，*你为什么不穿上呢*？”

En: I'm just that rugged.
Zh: “我就是这么强壮。”

En: It's part of my penance.
Zh: “这是我救赎的一部分。”

En: It's a fashion thing. They're not bad enough for this stud.
Zh: “这是时尚，对本风流男子来说它们不够屌。”

En: Suit yourself. The lieutenant knocks some ash from his cigarette. "But you should probably consider wearing them anyway."
Zh: “请随意。”警督弹了弹烟灰，“但还是请你考虑把它们穿上。”

En: Now then... we should talk about the investigation -- but I also feel you're a bit *hazy* on the RCM. Our role here, our rights. Our *jurisdiction* basically. He lights a cigarette.
Zh: 他点燃香烟。“那么……我们应该聊聊调查的事——但我还是觉得你对RCM的概念有点*模糊*。我们在这里的角色，还有权力。主要说来，是我们的*管辖权*。”

En: I apologize, but I only brought one with me. I have exactly one cigarette every night while going over my notes.
Zh: “我很抱歉，但我身上只带了一根。每晚我复查笔记的时候，都只会抽上一根。”

En: Your call, detective. Again, I only have *exactly* one every night. He blows a steady stream of smoke.
Zh: 他长长地吐出一团烟雾。“你决定吧，警探。再重申一次，我每晚*只*抽一根。”

En: Below, the city trembles with its remaining lights. People are rushing home, running away from home, smoking, sleeping, singing in the shower. Edges of dining tables, hands, aprons flicker through windows before the lights go out.
Zh: 下方，城市随着余下的灯火一起颤抖。人们赶回家，离开家，抽烟，睡觉，边洗澡边唱歌。在灯光熄灭之前，餐桌边缘、手还有围裙都在窗户里闪烁。

En: If you want to smoke like a rock and roll star, that's up to you. Again, I only have *exactly* one every night. He blows a steady stream of smoke.
Zh: 他长长地吐出一团烟雾。“如果你想像摇滚明星那样抽烟，那取决于你自己。再重申一次，我每晚*只*抽一根。”

En: I think I might want to pick up smoking. Do you have any more cigarettes?
Zh: “我好像有点想抽烟。你还有烟吗？”

En: Oh man, he looks so devastatingly *cool* with that cigarette.
Zh: 天啦，他抽烟的样子真是*酷*到原地爆炸了。

En: I didn't know you smoked, Kim.
Zh: “我不知道你还会抽烟呢，金。”

En: How did you get so cool, Kim?
Zh: “你怎么这么酷，金？”

En: A cigarette is such a great idea. I think I'm going to have one too.
Zh: “抽烟真是个好主意。我觉得我也要来一根。”

En: You mean this? The light of his cigarette illuminates a fleeting smile. "This isn't cool -- it's an unnecessary trial of will. And unhealthy." He flicks the ash.
Zh: 他手中香烟的火光照亮了一个转瞬即逝的微笑。“你说这个吗？”他掸了掸烟灰。“这个不酷——这是对意志的一种不必要的考验。而且很不健康。”

En: I have a cigarette every night when I go over my notes. It's something of a ritual.
Zh: “我每晚复查笔记的时候都会抽上一根。就像某种仪式。”

En: Keeping the habit within the parameters he's given himself takes a lot of focus. It would be easier to simply quit.
Zh: 把习惯控制在自己设定的范围之内需要很强的自控力。相比之下直接戒烟可容易多了。

En: Yet were he to quit, he would lose the cool factor. This man relishes his cool quite a bit -- below it all.
Zh: 然而如果戒掉，他就酷不起来了。隐藏在这一切之下的——是这个男人很享受这种酷劲。

En: Right then -- the debrief. (Return to it.)
Zh: “那好吧——行动总结。”（回归主题。）

En: Yes, he pulls on the cigarette. "It's been a long and eventful day. "
Zh: “是的，”他抽了一口烟。“今天真是漫长又多事啊。”

En: And then there's the fact that he looks *way* cool doing it.
Zh: 事实上，这样做的他看起来确实酷*多了*。

En: The lights of the orphan district are reflected in his glasses: the red and golden orbs of the motorway sliding like pearls on a string, from East to West, as Revachol commutes back to the suburbs. Tomorrow is Tuesday. Monday is over.
Zh: 孤儿区的灯光映照在他的眼镜上：高速公路上红色和金色的光球宛如细绳上的珍珠一般从东向西滑动，瑞瓦肖的通勤路线又回到了郊外的位置。明天是星期二，星期一已经结束了。

En: Yes. It's part of the Jamrock shuffle.
Zh: “是的。这是加姆洛克开箱步的一部分。”

En: Thank you for the compliment. We could manage it even in wooden clogs. There are uncanny running-reservoirs in this body -- god knows why...
Zh: 谢谢你的赞美。即使穿着木屐，我们也能做到。这个身体里面有一台正在运转的神秘油箱——天知道到底是为什么……

En: He's right. There it is, green and shiny under the glowing Whirling window... The sky above is dark and the lights sparkle.
Zh: 他说得对。它就在那里，在飞旋旅社闪亮的窗户下散发着幽幽绿光……天空漆黑一片，只有灯光还在闪闪烁烁。

En: He has internalized it well. It's just that. A fact. A self-contained, past event.
Zh: 他已经把这件事在心里很好的消化掉了。这就是事实。一个独立的、过去的事件。

En: There is a *slight* physical revulsion in him while saying that, but he's worked through it in the intervening years.
Zh: 他说出这句话的时候，身体上反映出*一点点*的厌恶感，但是在这些年里，他一直在克服这个问题。

En: Let's say it was the citizens of Revachol.
Zh: “我说是瑞瓦肖的市民。”

En: Let's say it was the Coalition Government.
Zh: “我说是联盟政府。”

En: Be sentimental, if you like. Either way -- the Moralintern leases us the right to keep the peace in this city. And they will take it away if we misuse it.
Zh: “如果你喜欢，感性一点也可以。不管怎么说——我们维持这座城市和平的权力是国际道德伦理委员会*租*给我们的。如果我们滥用的话，他们就会把这种权力夺走。”

En: It's probably more honest, yes. Either way, the Moralintern leases us the right to keep the peace in this city. And they will take it away if we misuse it.
Zh: “没错，这样也许更诚实一些。不管怎么说——国际道德伦理委员会租给我们维持这座城市和平的权力。如果我们滥用的话，他们就会把这种权力夺走。”

En: Or if they *think* you do.
Zh: 或者，如果他们*觉得*你有的话。

En: The Moralintern -- what is it?
Zh: “国际道德伦理委员会——到底是什么？”

En: The Moralist International are the world's largest political organization. He pulls on his cigarette. "You know who they are. They have been running this place after the Revolution failed."
Zh: “国际道德伦理委员会是世界上最大的政治组织。”他抽了一口烟。“你知道他们是谁。在大革命失败之后，这个地方就交给他们管制了。”

En: What do they believe in?
Zh: “他们信仰什么？”

En: What is their *symbol*?
Zh: “他们的*符号*是什么？”

En: Who was Dolores Dei?
Zh: “德洛莉丝·黛是谁？”

En: Things are bad out there. (Point to the city.) "We need them here, giving us the right to police Revachol."
Zh: “外面的情况很糟糕。”（指向城市。）“我们需要他们，赋予我们管辖瑞瓦肖的权力。”

En: What do they believe in? They are Dolorians. They *believe* they continue the humanist project set forth by Her Innocence Dolores Dei four centuries ago. Others say they're just technocrats.
Zh: “他们有什么信仰？他们是德洛莉丝主义者。他们*相信*自己可以继续执行四个世纪前无罪女王德洛莉丝·黛提出的人道主义计划。其他人说他们只是一群科技主义者。”

En: Interesting question. It's a blue forget-me-not. Their motto is: Love, Compassion, Self-Discipline. I think you can gauge what they *want* you to think of them from that.
Zh: “这是个有趣的问题。是一支蓝色的勿忘我花。箴言是：爱，怜悯，自律。我觉得从这一点上，你就能判断出他们*希望*自己在你眼中是什么形象。”

En: Something kind and *usual*.
Zh: 某种友好和*平常*的东西。

En: Something almost self-explanatory.
Zh: 某种几乎可以说是不言而喻的东西。

En: Something even a little feminine, but in a *strict* manner.
Zh: 某种甚至有点女权主义的东西，但方式更加*严格*一些。

En: Something like the dark blue, serious colour of the early night sky above.
Zh: 某种深蓝色的东西，就像黄昏时天空那片肃穆的色彩。

En: Something ominous.
Zh: 某种不祥的东西。

En: A historic figure? The author of the modern age? He thinks. "You will have to look elsewhere for opinions. The subject of humanism is too abstract for me."
Zh: “一个历史人物？现代文明的奠基者？”他想到。“这个你还是去问问别人的意见吧。人道主义的课题对我来说太抽象了。”

En: For you, she is something painful, though it's hard to say why.
Zh: 对于你来说，她是某种痛苦的东西，虽然很难说到底是为什么。

En: They've done an awful job here. Have you seen the place? Things are bad.
Zh: “他们在这里干得很糟糕。你有没有看见这个地方？情况真的挺严重。”

En: They've done an awful job here. Have you seen the place? This isn't humanism.
Zh: “他们在这里干得很糟糕。你看到这个地方了吗？这一点也不人道。”

En: I have an *opinion* on the Moralintern. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我对这个国际道德伦理委员会有点*意见*。”（做结论。）

En: What do you think of them?
Zh: “你怎么看待他们？”

En: The Moralintern are a fact. I try not to have opinions on facts -- until they change. And, he looks at the city below, "it doesn't look like that's about to happen."
Zh: “国际道德伦理委员会是一个既成的事实。我不会对事实发表任何意见——除非它们改变了。而且……”他看着脚下的城市……“改变看起来也不太可能发生。”

En: It's more than that. There's some kind of affection in him.
Zh: 绝不仅仅如此，在他心里有着某种喜爱之情。

En: You like the Moralintern.
Zh: “你喜欢国际道德伦理委员会。”

En: Better not to ask.
Zh: 最好还是别问了。

En: The lieutenant breathes out a chestnut-scented cloud of cigarette smoke and looks at the city he's sworn to guard.
Zh: 警督吐出一团栗子味的烟雾，然后看着这座他发誓要守护的城市。

En: Yes. I *did* -- when I was younger. In my twenties I considered myself a moralist. *A blue forget-me-not, a piece of the sky*, he quotes. "They're not all that bad."
Zh: “是的。我*以前*是——在我比较年轻的时候。20多岁的时候我觉得自己是个道德家。*一支蓝色勿忘我花，一片蔚蓝的天空*，”他引用到。“其实也没那么糟糕。”

En: But the years have changed that. I don't know *what* I believe in now.... He thinks, then changes his mind. "No. I believe in the RCM. That's enough for me."
Zh: “但是岁月改变了一切。现在我也不知道自己相信*什么*……”他想了一会儿，然后改变了主意。“不。我相信RCM。对我来说这就够了。”

En: That's another leitmotiv associated with moralism.
Zh: 这又是一个与道德主义相关的主题。

En: Do you? The lieutenant arches his brow, then pulls on his cigarette. It's a slim white thing in his fingers.
Zh: “是吗？”警督皱起眉头，然后抽了一口烟。在他的指尖，那只是一根细长的白色玩意。

En: On second thought -- I *don't* have an opinion. Forget about it.
Zh: “又想了一下——我*没*什么意见。还是忘了吧。”

En: We are stooges of the world's biggest bourgeois organization, protecting bourgeois rights.
Zh: “我们是世界上最大的资产阶级组织的傀儡，为了保护资产阶级的权利。”

En: No. We are stooges of the world's biggest bourgeois organization -- protecting the people of Revachol. That's the hand we were dealt. Without the MI, we would be common vigilantes.
Zh: “不对。我们是世界上最大的资产阶级组织的傀儡——为了保护瑞瓦肖的人民。这才是我们要处理的问题。如果没有委员会，我们也许只是普通的义务警员。”

En: Mutter silently: "Immigrants, liberal kipts, fucking... men are turning into women."
Zh: 默默地嘀咕：“移民，自由主义的吉皮特，该死的……男人正在变成女人。”

En: In fact, we would need them even if you *didn't* think that way. We are in what is called the *twilight of international law*. The laws we claim to enforce come from the MI. Without them, we are simply vigilantes.
Zh: “事实上，就算你*不认同*，我们也是需要他们的。我们处于所谓的*国际法的暮光*之中，执行的法律也来自于委员会。没有他们，我们不过是普通的义务警员罢了。”

En: We are in what is called the *twilight of international law*. The laws we claim to enforce come from the MI. Without them, we are simply vigilantes.
Zh: “我们处于所谓的*国际法的暮光*之中，执行的法律也来自于委员会。没有他们，我们不过是普通的义务警员罢了。”

En: What happens to the people we convict?
Zh: “那被我们判处有罪的人会怎么样？”

En: We don't convict. We arrest and send them to Coalition Government courts -- in Couron and La Delta. The prosecution works off our testimonies. And records -- which is why it's paramount to keep them. He taps on his coat pocket -- where he keep his notes.
Zh: “判处罪行不是我们的工作。我们只负责逮捕，然后把他们送到联盟政府法庭——在科戎和德尔塔。控方会根据我们的证据行事。还有记录——这就是保护好记录的重要性所在。”他拍了拍上衣口袋——笔记就放在那里。

En: Well phrased. He nods and breathes out. "I understand answering to foreign powers doesn't sit well with immigrant-bashing -- but without the MI we would be common vigilantes. That's just the way it is."
Zh: “这个词用得好，”他点点头，呼出一口气。“我能理解，适应外国势力跟打击移民并不相符——但是如果没了委员会，我们只不过是普通的义务警员而已。事实就是如此。”

En: My kind of police officer, he nods. A light smile. Then looks at the roundabout.
Zh: “我们是一类警察，”他点点头，微微一笑，然后又看向环岛。

En: Actually, vigilantes is okay with me.
Zh: “其实，我对义务警员没什么意见。”

En: Yeah. Vigilantes sounds bad.
Zh: “是啊。义务警员听起来挺糟糕的。”

En: Maybe we should make our own law?
Zh: “也许我们应该制定自己的法律？”

En: Spoken like a revolutionary, not a cop. But hypothetical aside -- in Martinaise we already *are* vigilantes. At least the Union thinks so. I expect our job here to prove quite challenging. He looks at the roundabout.
Zh: “你这话听起来像个革命者，而不是一个警察。先把假设放在一边——我们在马丁内斯已经*是*义务警员了。至少工会是这么觉得的。我估计我们在这里的工作会很有挑战性。”他看向环岛。

En: Then you will *adore* Martinaise. For many of these people -- the Union especially -- vigilantes is precisely what we are. Personally, I don't enjoy it much. He looks at the roundabout.
Zh: “那你一定会*喜欢*马丁内斯的。对于很多人——特别是工会来说——我们其实就是义务警员。以我个人的角度来讲，我不是很喜欢这样。”他看向环岛。

En: He nods. "Sadly, it is what we already are to the people of Martinaise. Most of them at least, especially the Union -- vigilantes. I expect our job here to prove quite challenging." He looks at the roundabout.
Zh: 他点点头。“可悲的是，对于马丁内斯的人来说，我们已经是了。至少他们中的大部分人，特别是工会——觉得我们就是义务警员。我估计我们在这里的工作会很有挑战性。”他看向环岛。

En: The lights of the orphan district are reflected in his glasses: the red and golden orbs of the motorway sliding like pearls on a string, from East to West, as Revachol commutes back to the suburbs. Tomorrow is Tuesday. Monday is over.
Zh: 孤儿区的灯光映照在他的眼镜上：高速公路上红色和金色的光球宛如细绳上的珍珠一般，从东向西滑动。瑞瓦肖的通勤路线又回到了郊外。明天是星期二，星期一已经结束了。

En: The dying lights of the city shimmer below -- slowly, like luminous clouds, they pass on his lenses. The lieutenant looks at his slim cigarette, contemplating the next drag.
Zh: 城市的微光在下面慢慢闪烁——就像发光的云彩，从他的镜片上飘过。警督看着手中细长的香烟，又深吸了一口。

En: They *really* don't like us here. And the mouth on that kid, Cuno... It's different inland. In Jamrock and the G.R.I.H. He pulls on the cigarette.
Zh: 最后他抽了一口烟，说道：“他们*真的*不喜欢我们在这里。还有那孩子的话，坤诺……来自不同的内陆。在加姆洛克和G.R.I.H.”

En: This *soldier* is the highlight of the day for me, he thinks. This little stick right here.
Zh: 对于我来说，这个*士兵*是今天最精彩的部分，他想到。就是这个小玩意。

En: An aerostatic passes overhead -- the whiskers of its floodlights on the ground below. Kitsuragi's glasses light up as he looks to the sky. Two glowing circles.
Zh: 一艘静风舰驶过头顶——强光灯的触手伸向下方的土地。曷城看着天空，镜片也随之被点亮，像两个发光的圆环。

En: They really don't like us here. The Union, the teenagers too... It's different inland. In Jamrock and the G.R.I.H. He pulls on the cigarette.
Zh: 他抽了一口烟，说道：“他们*真的*不喜欢我们在这里。还有那孩子的话，坤诺……是不同的内陆，在加姆洛克和G.R.I.H.”

En: It's our fault for leaving this place to the dogs. To the Union. To the company. Not daring to come here more often. It's like I told you -- this place is an orphan. Fallen between the cracks...
Zh: “把这个地方留给那群野狗是我们的失误。留给工会，还有公司。谁都不敢经常来这里逗留，就像我告诉过你的——这个地方就是个孤儿区。在裂隙中陷落……”

En: Why *are* they like this?
Zh: “他们为什么*会是*这个样子？”

En: And in Jamrock and the G.R.I.H? (Continue.)
Zh: “加姆洛克和G.R.I.H.也是吗？”（继续。）

En: It's our fault for leaving this place to the dogs. To the Union. To the company. Not daring to come here more often. This place has fallen between the cracks. The jurisdictions of our two Precincts.
Zh: “把这个地方留给那群野狗是我们的失误。留给工会，还有公司。谁都不敢经常来这里。这个地方在裂隙中陷落了，是我们俩辖区的共同管辖区。”

En: We run this city. West of the river is RCM land. He looks at the dark silhouette of the equestrian monument cutting into the night sky.
Zh: 他看着夜空中骑马者纪念碑的黑色轮廓，然后说道：“这座城市由我们管辖。河流西边是RCM的领地。”

En: It's *incredibly* hard. Human beings are... He shakes his head. "But we are in control. And it's worth it. The organization works. Our systems work. If they didn't, this city would disintegrate."
Zh: “其实*非常*困难。人类实在是……”他摇摇头。“但一切都在我们的管控之中。而且这都是值得的。组织起作用了，我们的系统也在起作用。如果没有的话，这座城市会分崩离析。”

En: Night. Captain Ptolemy Pryce steps into the yard. A piebald horse waits by the motor carriage, chewing oats out of an oat-bag. Seagulls fly overhead. The sky is black. Captain Pryce wears a black suit and a standard patrol coat as he mounts the horse to head home...
Zh: 夜晚。托勒密·普赖斯警长走入院子。一匹花斑马在汽車旁等待着，咀嚼着袋子里的燕麦。海鸥从头顶飞过，整个天空漆黑一片。普赖斯警长穿着一套黑西服，披着一件标准的巡逻大衣，骑在马上走向回家的方向……

En: Rows of houses on either side, hunching over the sidewalks -- and Precinct 41 with its dome roof growing distant. Around him the streets are silent. A kid on the corner waves at the captain as he takes the turn on Perdition and Main. The horse neighs. The captain nods back.
Zh: 两边是一排排的屋子，伫立在人行道的两旁——41分局的圆顶向远处慢慢延伸。他周围的街道寂静无声。一个小孩经过永劫路和主路的转角，朝着警长的方向挥了挥手。马发出一声嘶鸣，警长点头示意。

En: Thanks, kid, he thinks. He's grateful.
Zh: 谢谢了，孩子，他想到，心中充满感激。

En: They're a union of centre-left and centre-right parties across the Reál Belt. Our Coalition Government is just one of its many projects. They also run the ICP, EPIS... most inter-governmental organizations in the world.
Zh: “他们是中左翼党派和中右翼党派的联合体，因为雷亚尔而维系在一起。我们的联盟政府只是很多很多计划中的一个。他们还运营着ICP，EPIS……世界上大部分政府间组织。”

En: I really don't know. That's how bad it is in here. (Point to your head.)
Zh: “我真的不知道。这里的情况就真有这么糟糕。”（指着你的头。）

En: If I *didn't* know -- how would you describe them?
Zh: “如果我*不知道*——你会怎么描述他们？”

En: Those others say *they* continue the humanist project set forth by Dolores Dei.
Zh: 其他人说*他们*继续着德洛莉丝·黛提出的人道主义计划。

En: Say nothing. Just look into the night.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是看着这片夜色。

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Silence -- a great comment to such a conundrum.
Zh: “沉默——对于这种难题来说，是很好的评价。”

En: Okay. They're a union of centre-left and centre-right parties across the Reál Belt. Our Coalition Government is just one of its many projects. They also run the ICP, EPIS... most inter-governmental organizations in the world.
Zh: “好吧。他们是中左翼党派和中右翼党派的联合体，因为雷亚尔而维系在一起。我们的联盟政府只是很多很多计划中的一个。他们还运营着ICP，EPIS……世界上的大部分政府间组织。”

En: Thank you for this. [Leave.]
Zh: “谢谢你。”[离开。]

En: Well, we didn't really look at the body... The lieutenant raises his eyebrows.
Zh: 警督抬起眉头。“呃，我们甚至都没怎么看那具尸体……”

En: How do you think today went?
Zh: “你觉得今天的进展如何？”

En: Well, you were so hungover that you couldn't keep it down when we approached the hanged man, so we didn't even inspect the body.
Zh: “呃，你宿醉得很厉害，我们靠近那个上吊男人的时候，你根本控制不了自己，所以我们都没能检查那具尸体。”

En: Well, we didn't really look at the body... The lieutenant raises his eyebrows.
Zh: 警督扬起眉头。“呃，我们的确还没看过那具尸体……”

En: Well, we didn't even look at the body... The lieutenant raises his eyebrows.
Zh: 警督抬起眉头。“呃，我们甚至都没怎么看那具尸体……”

En: We performed a thorough initial inspection of the victim's body. So that's good. One could say it's the main thing to do in a murder.
Zh: “我们对受害人进行了详尽的初步检查。这一点很好。有人会说这是凶杀案调查中最主要的事情。”

En: We performed a cursory initial inspection of the victim's body.
Zh: “我们对受害人的尸体进行了初略检查。”

En: Our initial inspection of the victim's body wasn't as thorough as it might have been.
Zh: “我们对受害人尸体的初步检查并不彻底，本不该这样的。”

En: Well, we inspected the victim's body. So that's good -- it was not easily *approachable* in that state, but we did it.
Zh: “嗯，我们检查了被害人的尸体。这挺好的——那种状态可不容易*让人接近*，但我们还是做到了。”

En: Then you shot him in the chest, which... wasn't ideal. And we still have to get him down from the tree.
Zh: “然后你开枪击中了他的胸部，这个……不是很理想。不过我们还是得想办法把他从树上弄下来。”

En: Then you shot the body down, which was... He pauses, searching for the right word. "...quite the shot."
Zh: “然后你开枪把尸体打了下来，就还……”他停顿了一下，想要寻找最合适的词语。“……打的挺准的。”

En: Then you shot him in the chest, which... wasn't ideal. But we did manage to get him down from the tree. So, as they say, all's well that ends well.
Zh: “然后你开枪击中了他的胸部，这个……不是很理想。不过总算把他从树上弄下来了。所以，俗话说得好，结果好一切都好。”

En: Our inspection was also very *thorough* -- and we've already started following up on leads.
Zh: “我们的检查非常的*彻底*——而且我们已经开始追踪线索了。”

En: I would say our initial inspection was *very* thorough -- and we have solid leads to follow up on.
Zh: “我会说我们的初步检查非常的*彻底*——而且现在已经有可靠的线索可以跟进了。”

En: Our inspection left something to be desired and didn't produce enough solid leads, though.
Zh: “我们的调查还有一些需要改进的地方，不过也没能提供什么足够可靠的线索。”

En: Our inspection *could* have been more thorough -- as it always can -- but we have some leads we can follow up on.
Zh: “我们的检查*可以*再详细一点——因为总是可以的——不过，我们手上确实找到一些可以跟进的线索。”

En: Moreover, you found that the hanged man wasn't just hanged -- he was also shot. That was some *excellent* detective work.
Zh: “而且，你发现那个男人不仅仅被吊起来了——还中枪了。这次勘查工作完成的*相当不错*。”

En: *And* you managed to locate and pull out the bullet. So we can get ballistics, make of the gun -- all this is invaluable.
Zh: “*而且*，你还找到了一颗子弹。所以我们可以拿去做弹道学测试，查出作案手枪——这些信息都是非常重要的。”

En: Yeah, I'm a professional.
Zh: “是啊，我可是专业人士。”

En: No big deal.
Zh: “没什么大不了的。”

En: It was the least I could do, given all my past mistakes.
Zh: “考虑到我过去犯下的错误，这是我唯一能做的。”

En: So what are our powers exactly? The RCM?
Zh: “那我们的权力到底是什么呢？我是说RCM？”

En: I hope our investigation will help improve the situation here. At least do *some* good.
Zh: “希望我们的调查可以帮助改善这里的情况。至少能产生*一些*好影响。”

En: We won't change anything here.
Zh: “我们改变不了什么。”

En: What the people need is stronger leadership that would put everyone in line.
Zh: “人们需要的是更强的领导力，能让所有人都安分守己。”

En: Me too, he says quietly. "But I wouldn't count on any drastic changes in our lifetimes."
Zh: “我也是，”他小声说道，“但是我不会指望我们的人生会因此改变很多。”

En: Probably not, he says quietly. "Anyway, positive change happens slowly. We never really get to see the impact of our actions."
Zh: “也许不会，”他小声说道。“不管怎么说，积极的变化总是缓慢发生的。我们从来没有真正看到过自身行动带来的影响。”

En: That seems unwise to me, but it's too late for an all-out political debate. He purses his lips.
Zh: 他撇了撇嘴。“在我看来不太明智，不过现在来一场全面的政治辩论太迟了。”

En: Now, as for interviews -- my list of people to talk to here in Martinaise, I mean... He takes a deep drag and looks at the city.
Zh: “现在，说说走访的事——我这里有一份马丁内斯的走访清单，我的意思是……”他深吸一口气，然后看着这座城市。

En: We conducted an interview with Evrart Claire.
Zh: “我们走访了艾弗拉特·克莱尔。”

En: We couldn't conduct an interview with Evrart Claire because you refused to take a seat.
Zh: “我们没能走访艾弗拉特·克莱尔，因为你拒绝坐下。”

En: We met Evrart Claire, but you left the conversation abruptly before we could really interview him.
Zh: “我们见到了艾弗拉特·克莱尔，但是在真正开始询问前，你很突然地离开了谈话。”

En: We met Evrart Claire, but didn't conduct a full interview. So that's one for the to-do list...
Zh: “我们见到了艾弗拉特·克莱尔，但是没能进行完整的询问。所以这个要放进待办清单里……”

En: The body is still hanging from that tree, which is... unfortunate. But we performed a thorough search of the premises of the crime scene -- that's great.
Zh: “尸体还挂在树上，这一点有些……遗憾。不过我们对于犯罪现场进行了详尽的搜查——这一点非常棒。”

En: The body is still hanging from that tree, which is... unfortunate. And there's still *much* to do at the crime scene.
Zh: “尸体还挂在那棵树上，还蛮……不幸的。而且犯罪现场还有*很多*事要做。”

En: I still feel like we're missing something. But we performed a thorough search of the premises of the crime scene. That's great.
Zh: “我还是觉得我们遗漏了什么。不过我们对犯罪现场进行了详尽的搜查。这一点非常棒。”

En: I still feel like we're missing something. And there's still work to do at the crime scene too...
Zh: “我还是觉得我们遗漏了什么。而且犯罪现场还有很多事要做……”

En: I still feel like we missed something, but at least the corpse has been refrigerated. And we performed a thorough search of the premises of the crime scene. That's great.
Zh: “我还是觉得我们遗漏了什么，不过至少尸体已经被冷冻起来了。而且我们还对犯罪现场进行了详尽的搜查。这一点非常棒。”

En: I still feel like we missed something, but at least the corpse has been refrigerated. There's still work to be done at the crime scene, though.
Zh: “我还是觉得我们遗漏了什么，但是至少尸体已经冷冻起来了。不过犯罪现场还有很多事要做。”

En: I still feel like we missed something, but it's up to the boys in Processing now. We did perform a thorough search of the crime scene. That's great.
Zh: “我还是觉得我们遗漏了什么，不过现在只能指望分析部门的那些小伙子了。我们还对犯罪现场进行了详尽的搜查。这一点非常棒。”

En: I still feel like we missed something, but maybe the boys in Processing will surprise us. We still have work to do at the crime scene.
Zh: “我还是觉得我们遗漏了什么，不过也许分析部的人会带给我们什么惊喜。我们在犯罪现场还有很多事要处理。”

En: We didn't get to meet Evrart Claire, but we did get into the harbour. It'll be easier to reach him tomorrow.
Zh: “我们没能见到艾弗拉特·克莱尔，不过确实进入港口了。明天应该更容易找到他。”

En: We weren't able to find the Union leader, Evrart Claire, much less interview him. So that's on the to-do list for tomorrow.
Zh: “我们没能找到工会主席，艾弗拉特·克莱尔，更别提走访他了。所以，这件事放到了明天的待办清单上。”

En: We interviewed Joyce Messier, the Wild Pines rep, and learned a lot -- none of it very *good news* for us or Martinaise, but... He gives you a quick nod of acknowledgement and approval.
Zh: “我们走访了野松公司的代表乔伊斯·梅西耶，而且了解到很多信息——对于我们或者马丁内斯来说，没有一条算得上是*好消息*，不过……”他快速点了点头，表示赞同和认可。

En: We tried to interview the Wild Pines rep, but she asked us to do something for her first. Fine. So be it.
Zh: “我们试着去走访野松公司的代表，但是她要我们先为她办点事。好吧，那就这样做吧。”

En: We talked to Joyce Messier, but didn't get any information from her. He frowns. "I have a feeling Joyce knows how dangerous the situation really is. We *have* to get her to talk to us."
Zh: “我们跟乔伊斯·梅西耶聊了聊，但没有从她那里得到任何信息。”他皱了皱眉头。“我感觉乔伊斯知道现在的情况到底有多危险。我们*必须*想办法让她开口。”

En: We didn't talk to the Wild Pines rep. We really must do that tomorrow.
Zh: “我们没有跟野松公司的代表谈话。不过这绝对是明天的必办事项。”

En: Above all, though... today was exhausting. What's with all the *running*? You run a lot. Is that a standard Precinct 41 practice?
Zh: “不过首先我想说……今天真是累坏了。这么到处*跑来跑去*是要做什么？你跑了很久。这是41分局的标准训练吗？”

En: Yeah, that's just how we roll in Precinct 41.
Zh: “是啊，我们41局就是这么来的。”

En: I'm training for a long-distance run. I want to raise money for charity.
Zh: “我在训练长跑。我想为慈善事业筹集资金。”

En: I don't like to waste time, you know. My mind moves fast -- the rest has to try to keep up.
Zh: “你知道的，我不喜欢浪费时间。我的脑子动的更快——其他部分都有点跟不上了。”

En: I don't know why I do the things I do, Lieutenant Kitsuragi.
Zh: “我不知道自己为什么会这么做，曷城警督。”

En: It's impressive, especially for a man your age -- and in *those* heels... He nods thoughtfully, tapping his finger on his cigarette.
Zh: 他若有所思地点点头，用手指轻轻弹了下香烟。“还挺少见的，特别是你这个年纪的男人——而且还穿着*那些*高跟鞋……”

En: Where shall we begin? We should talk about the investigation first and foremost, but I also remember you wanting to discuss the RCM? He lights a cigarette.
Zh: 他点燃一支香烟。“我们该从哪里开始呢？首先应该谈谈调查的事，不过我记得你想聊一下RCM。”

En: They're quite limited, actually -- the power officers of the Revachol Citizens Militia exercise most *frequently* is imposing fines of up to 1000 reál for offences, in accordance with an interdepartmental schedule.
Zh: 其实很有限——最*经常*做的就是根据各部门罚款标准，对违法者处以最高1000雷亚尔的罚款。

En: A thousand? Why not more?
Zh: “1000？怎么不多一点？”

En: Wouldn't that be an easy power to... abuse?
Zh: “这不是很容易导致权力……滥用吗？”

En: Okay. What else? (Continue.)
Zh: “好吧。还有什么？”（继续。）

En: Yes, although indirectly, as citizens can always request records from their local station.
Zh: “是的，虽然是间接的，不过市民总是可以从当地的警局索要记录。”

En: Officers of the RCM have been known to take bribes of less than the prescribed fine amount. He frowns. "It undermines trust in the RCM."
Zh: “RCM的警官收受少于规定罚款金额的贿赂是众所周知的。”他皱起眉头。“这样会逐渐破坏RCM的公信力。”

En: The RCM's primary role is to ensure safety. We're not really supposed to play any part in the economic structure of Revachol.
Zh: “RCM扮演的主要角色是保障安全。我们真的不应该在瑞瓦肖的经济结构中扮演任何角色。”

En: We can arrest people, of course, but, rather than bringing someone in directly, it's preferable to serve a *Station Call* slip. It prevents confusion and overcrowding.
Zh: “当然了，我们可以逮捕人，不过相比直接抓人，更好的选择是*警局传唤通知*。这样可以避免混乱和过度拥挤。”

En: Wait, how can you be sure the arrestee will show up?
Zh: “等等，那你怎么确定被捕对象会出现呢？”

En: I see. And if someone resists?
Zh: “我明白了。那如果有人反抗呢？”

En: You can't. Those who don't show up become fugitives though, and have fewer legal rights when they are eventually caught. It's about power projection. Thus far they seem to mostly show up.
Zh: “确定不了。不过那些没出现的人会变成逃犯，当他们最后被抓到的时候，享有的合法权利也会比较少。这其实是一种权力投射。到目前为止，好像大部分人都会现身。”

En: When power calls you, you come. But power itself is a fragile trick of perception.
Zh: 在当权者召唤你的时候，你就会来。但是权力本身只是一种脆弱的感官把戏。

En: As you may have gathered from the fact that we are expected to carry a record of our kills -- like the one in your watermarks -- we're permitted to use whatever force is necessary. And strongly admonished not to abuse that power.
Zh: “你可能已经从事实中了解到，我们需要随身携带击杀人数的记录——就像你水印里的那个——我们可以使用任何必要的武力。而且，我们也被严肃地告诫过，不要滥用这种权力。”

En: We are permitted to use whatever force we deem necessary. Even lethal.
Zh: “我们被允许使用任何我们认为有必要的武力，甚至包括致命的。”

En: Wait, so if I kill someone while on duty...
Zh: “等等，所以说如果我在执行任务的时候杀了人……”

En: Have you ever killed anyone, Kim?
Zh: “你杀过人吗，金？”

En: Have *I* killed anyone?
Zh: “那*我*有杀过人吗？”

En: You have to supply compelling evidence for why it was necessary to use lethal force. In these cases -- your partner is usually your witness.
Zh: “你必须为使用致命武力的必要性提供令人信服的证据。在这种状况下——一般你的搭档就是你的证人。”

En: Yes. He pauses. "The last time was four years ago."
Zh: “有过。”他停顿了一下。“上次已经是四年前的事了。”

En: That's an improbability -- perhaps even impossibility...
Zh: “那个不大可能——又或许是完全不可能……”

En: *Not* a good position to be in, by the way. Internal Affairs handles these cases thoroughly -- by cross-examining you for inconsistencies. It is hard to *cover* for anyone. Which is for the best.
Zh: “顺便说一句，处于那种位置可*不是*很舒服的。内务部会彻查这些案子——详细盘问你所有矛盾的地方。要想为别人*打掩护*是很难的。其实这样最好。”

En: He says it matter-of-factly and moves on.
Zh: 他实事求是的说完，然后离开了。

En: Of course you've killed people. It's somewhere down there, melting in all the drink...
Zh: 你当然杀过人。就在这附近的某个地方，融化在所有酒精里……

En: I must have. I feel it in me.
Zh: “我肯定有过。我感觉到了。”

En: I probably have.
Zh: “我也许有过。”

En: I don't know if I really want to know.
Zh: “我不确定自己是不是真想知道。”

En: No. I don't think I have.
Zh: “不。我觉得我没有。”

En: Precinct 41 is known for... a higher-than-average rate of police violence.
Zh: “41分局最出名的就是……警察暴力事件远超平均水平。”

En: Perhaps. But Precinct 41 is known for... a higher-than-average rate of kills. He raises his eyebrows.
Zh: 他扬起眉毛。“也许吧。不过41分局最出名的就是……远超平均水平的击杀人数。”

En: He's not judging you. He's respectfully acknowledging the difficulty of working in your precinct.
Zh: 他不是在评判你。他郑重地承认你们分局的工作难度。

En: And it's the Coalition Government that makes all these rules? (Continue.)
Zh: “那这些规则是联盟政府制定的吗？”（继续。）

En: And who makes all these rules? The Coalition Government? (Continue.)
Zh: “这些规则是谁制定的？联盟政府吗？”（继续。）

En: And who makes all these rules? (Continue.)
Zh: “那这些规则都是谁制定的？”（继续。）

En: And the Moralintern more broadly, but yes. His gaze is absently fixed on a window below that just went dark.
Zh: “更宽泛的说，是国际道德伦理委员，不过你说的没错。”他的目光心不在焉地落在刚刚变黑的窗户上。

En: The Coalition Government and the Moralintern more broadly.
Zh: “更宽泛的说，是联盟政府和国际道德伦理委员会。”

En: The RCM was formed by the Coalition Government to restore order in the International Zone after the Revolution. So we did. Now we attempt to *maintain* that order, no more, no less. His gaze is absently fixed on a window below that just went dark.
Zh: “RCM是联盟政府成立的，为的就是恢复大革命后国际区域的秩序。所以我们就这么做了。现在我们试着*维持*这个秩序，一分不多，一分不少。”

En: Or... perhaps it is better to say we were *allowed to form*. It's a point of contention whether the citizens of Revachol or the Coalition Government founded the RCM.
Zh: 他的目光心不在焉地落在刚刚变黑的窗户上。“或者……应该说我们是*被允许成立*的更好。到底是瑞瓦肖人民，还是联盟政府成立了RCM，这是一个争论的焦点。”

En: You really got him talking, and now we know a lot about local politics, including Claire's ambitions and drug trade, and also have some viable suspects.
Zh: “你真的让他说了很多，现在我们对本地政治有了更多了解，包括克莱尔的野心还有毒品交易，而且还掌握了几个可靠的嫌疑人。”

En: You really got him talking, and now we know a lot about local politics, including Claire's drug trade, and also have some viable suspects.
Zh: “你真的让他说了很多，现在我们对本地政治有了更多了解，包括克莱尔的毒品交易，而且还掌握了几个可靠的嫌疑人。”

En: You really got him talking, and now we know a lot about the union, and also have some viable suspects.
Zh: “你真的让他说了很多，现在我们对工会有了更多了解，而且还掌握了几个可靠的嫌疑人。”

En: You got him to talk to you about both the Union and the murder, so we're definitely making progress there.
Zh: “关于工会还有那起凶杀案，你真的让他说了很多，所以我们的调查确实有了不小的进展。”

En: You got him to talk to you about the Union, but he wouldn't talk to you about the murder without having you do a favour for him first.
Zh: “你让他说了很多工会的事，但是除非你先帮他一个忙，要不他就会对凶杀案的事只字不提。”

En: You got him to talk to you about the Union and even his drug trade, but he wouldn't talk to you about the murder without having you do a favour for him first.
Zh: “你让他说了很多工会的事，甚至连毒品交易都说了。但是除非你先帮他一个忙，要不他就会对凶杀案的事只字不提。”

En: You got him to talk to you about the Union and even his drug trade, but he wouldn't talk to you about the murder.
Zh: “你让他说了很多工会的事，甚至连毒品交易都说了，但关于凶杀案的事，他只字未提。”

En: You got him to talk to you about the Union, but not the murder.
Zh: “你让他说了很多工会的事，但是凶杀案的事没提。”

En: Though he wasn't forthcoming about the Union, you got him talking about the murder. Good one, that.
Zh: “虽然他对工会的事不那么坦率，但确实提了凶杀案的事。这一点你做的不错。”

En: Though he wasn't forthcoming about the Union, you got him talking about the murder, and now we have some viable suspects.
Zh: “虽然他对工会的事不那么坦率，但确实提了凶杀案的事。所以现在我们手上有几个可靠的嫌疑人了。”

En: Though he wasn't forthcoming about the Union, you got him talking about the murder, and now we have some viable suspects. Moreover, we learned something about Claire's personal ambitions in all of this.
Zh: “虽然他对工会的事不那么坦率，但确实提了凶杀案的事。所以现在我们手上有几个可靠的嫌疑人了。除此之外，我们还从中了解到克莱尔个人的野心。”

En: He wasn't particularly forthcoming with useful information, however.
Zh: “不过，他对有用的信息都不是特别坦诚。”

En: Yeah, it's getting very cold now. Let's go. He puts out the stub of his cigarette and looks to the door.
Zh: “是啊，现在有点冷了。我们走吧。”他掐灭烟头，看着大门的方向。

En: He is very tired, but the dark circles under his eyes make him look younger, not older.
Zh: 他很疲惫，但眼睛下面的黑眼圈让他显得更加年轻，而不是更加苍老了。

En: I have a really good theory about why you're running so fast, son. Just you wait until we get up tomorrow!
Zh: 关于你为什么会跑的这么快这一点，我有个相当好的理论，孩子。等到明早起来之后再看吧！

En: And there's still much to do at the crime scene too. We didn't search it thoroughly enough.
Zh: “而且犯罪现场还有很多事要处理。我们调查地还不够彻底。”

En: But we performed a thorough search of the premises of the crime scene. That's great.
Zh: “不过我们已经对犯罪现场进行了详细的搜查。这一点很好。”

En: Though you really dropped the ball on inspecting the body, he doesn't want you to feel too discouraged. Probably out of fear that you'll just give up and keep drinking...
Zh: 虽然你确实在检查尸体的时候犯了错，但他不想让你感觉太灰心。也许是担心你会直接放弃，然后继续酗酒……

En: He would have put all of this more harshly, but he doesn't want you to feel completely discouraged. Probably because he's afraid that you'll just give up and keep drinking...
Zh: 他也许会更严厉地对待这一切，但也不想让你太灰心。也许是担心你会直接放弃，然后继续酗酒……

En: I generally prefer to start with searching the scene and then move on to dealing with dead bodies.
Zh: “我一般更喜欢从搜查现场开始，然后再去处理尸体。”

En: I'm sorry I couldn't do more today. I'm just not a very good cop, am I.
Zh: “很抱歉我今天做的还不够多。我真的不是个好警察。”

En: With the end being nigh, how can anyone expect me to focus on one corpse...
Zh: “世界末日都快到了，为什么还会有人期待我把注意力集中在一具尸体上面……”

En: I'm sorry I couldn't do more today. I'm just not a very good cop, am I.
Zh: “很抱歉我今天做的还不够多。我真的不是个好警察。”

En: It's all part of the plan -- starting from the outside and working back toward the scene of the murder.
Zh: “这都是计划的一部分——从外面开始，然后回到凶案现场。”

En: With the end being so *nigh*, how can anyone expect me to focus on one corpse?
Zh: “世界末日都*快到*了，为什么还会有人期待我把注意力集中在一具尸体上面……”

En: It's not my job to evaluate your methodology, officer. I'm just going through the facts. As for the interviews...
Zh: “我的工作不是评价你的方法论，警官。我只是在陈述事实。至于走访的事……”

En: He sighs. "We're both doing our best under the circumstances. Just... don't drink tonight, please. As for the interviews..."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“在这种环境下，我们都在竭尽全力。只是……麻烦你今晚别喝酒了。至于走访的事……”

En: He looks at you with an unreadable expression. "What if this murder is a portent? As for the interviews..."
Zh: 他看着你，脸上带着难以辨认的表情。“如果这起凶杀案是一个前兆呢？至于走访的事……”

En: He sighs. "We're both doing our best under the circumstances. Just... don't drink tonight, please. As for the interviews..."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“在这种环境下，我们都在竭尽全力。只是……麻烦你今晚别喝酒了。至于走访的事……”

En: It's not my job to evaluate your methodology, officer. I'm just going through the facts. As for the interviews...
Zh: “我的工作不是评价你的方法论，警官。我只是在检查事实。至于走访的事……”

En: He looks at you with an unreadable expression. "What if this murder is a portent? As for the interviews..."
Zh: 他看着你，脸上带着难以辨认的表情。“如果这起凶杀案是一个前兆呢？至于走访的事……”

En: Damn straight. I'm a sharpshooting cop.
Zh: “真够直接的。我是个枪法高超的警察。”

En: It was nothing, really. I was just doing my job.
Zh: “其实没什么，真的。我只是在做自己的本职工作而已。”

En: I was inspired by your confidence in me, Kim.
Zh: “你对我的信心给了我最大的鼓舞，金。”

En: I just can't wait to shoot more shit. I think I was born with a gun in my hands.
Zh: “我已经等不及要再开一枪了。我觉得自己生来手里就是带枪的。”

En: By *not ideal* he means he can't believe he let you try that trick and would be angry if he didn't feel the outcome was partly his responsibility.
Zh: 他说的那个*不理想*，意思是说他不相信自己居然真让你试了一下那个把戏，而且如果他不觉得这个后果也有自己一部分责任的话，一定会很生气的。

En: I just wanted to make sure he was really dead.
Zh: “我只想确认他真的死了。”

En: I admit, I'm a little out of practice.
Zh: “我承认，我是很久没有训练过了。”

En: I still can't believe I missed that shot!
Zh: “我还是不相信自己居然打偏了！”

En: He raises his eyebrows. "Right. As for the interviews..."
Zh: 他抬起眉毛。“好吧。至于走访的事……”

En: Being sober also tends to help with precision. But, moving on to the interviews...
Zh: “清醒同样也有助于提高精准度。不过，我们还是继续说说走访的事吧……”

En: Being sober tends to help with precision. But, moving on to the interviews...
Zh: “清醒同样也有助于提高精准度。不过，我们还是继续说说走访的事吧……”

En: On this occasion, I must agree. At any rate, your shot enabled us to perform a field autopsy on the victim. We found some things we can really work with.
Zh: “在这种情况下，我必须表示赞同。不管怎么说，你那一枪让我们能够对受害人进行现场尸检。我们发现了一些可以调查的线索。”

En: On this occasion, I must agree. At any rate, your shot enabled us to perform a field autopsy on the victim. We didn't find as much as I'd hoped.
Zh: “在这种情况下，我必须表示赞同。不管怎么说，你那一枪让我们能够对受害人进行现场尸检。不过，我们发现的东西没有我期望的多。”

En: On this occasion, I must agree. At any rate, your shot enabled us to perform a field autopsy on the victim. We didn't learn much, though.
Zh: “在这种情况下，我必须同意。无论如何，你那一枪让我们能够对受害者进行现场尸检。不过，得到的信息不是很多。”

En: We still have to perform the autopsy, though. However, we did a thorough search of the crime scene.
Zh: “我们还是要进行尸检。不过我们对犯罪现场进行了详尽的搜查。”

En: On this occasion, I must agree. We still have to perform the autopsy, though. And there's more work to be done at the crime scene.
Zh: “在这种情况下，我必须同意。不过我们还要进行尸检。而且犯罪现场还有很多工作等着我们呢。”

En: I admit, I wasn't sure whether I should give you the gun, but I'm glad I did. We still have to perform the autopsy, though. But we did do a thorough search of the crime scene -- that's great.
Zh: “我承认，我不确定该不该把枪给你，但是我很高兴自己这么做了。不过尸检还是要做的。但是我们对犯罪现场进行了详尽的搜查——这一点非常棒。”

En: I admit, I wasn't sure whether I should give you the gun, but I'm glad I did. We still have to perform the autopsy, though. And there's more work to be done at the crime scene.

En: It was something. We still have to perform a field autopsy, though. But we did do a thorough search of the crime scene -- that's great.
Zh: “的确挺不错的。不过尸检还是要做的。但是我们对犯罪现场进行了详尽的搜查——这一点非常棒。”

Zh: “确实挺不错的。不过我们还要进行现场尸检。而且，犯罪现场还有很多工作等着我们呢。”

En: I admit, I wasn't sure whether I should give you the gun, but I'm glad I did. Your shot enabled us to perform a field autopsy on the victim. Alas, we didn't find as much as I'd hoped.

En: I admit, I wasn't sure whether I should give you the gun, but I'm glad I did. Your shot enabled us to perform a field autopsy on the victim. I just wish we'd learned more.
Zh: “我承认，我不确定是不是应该把枪给你，但我很高兴自己这样做了。你那一枪让我们能够对受害者进行现场尸检。我只是希望能多了解一些信息就好了。”

En: It enabled us to perform a field autopsy on the victim. We found some things we can really work with.
Zh: “这让我们能对受害者进行现场尸检。而且我们也找到一些可以调查的线索。”

En: It enabled us to perform a field autopsy on the victim. We didn't find as much as I'd hoped.
Zh: “这让我们能对受害者进行现场尸检。不过，我们发现的东西没有我期望的多。”

Zh: “这让我们能对受害者进行现场尸检。不过，我们发现的东西没有我期望的多。”

En: It enabled us to perform a field autopsy on the victim. We didn't learn much, though.
Zh: “你那一枪让我们能够对受害者进行现场尸检。不过，我们没了解到多少信息。”

En: He's right. You could smell it, that something missing, even under the overpowering odour of the corpse.
Zh: 他是对的。即使是在尸体刺鼻的气味下，你也能嗅到，好像还少了点什么东西。

En: The rest is up to the boys in Processing. Maybe they will surprise us by doing their job for once, but I wouldn't count on it.
Zh: “剩下来的就交给分析部的那些小伙子了。也许他们这次能做好自己的工作，给我们个惊喜，不过我并没有抱太大指望。”

En: We haven't gotten the body out of the yard yet, but at least it's been refrigerated.
Zh: “我们还没有把尸体运出院子，不过至少他已经被冷冻起来了。”

En: Unfortunately, the body is still decomposing in the yard. We should take care of that as soon as possible.
Zh: “很可惜，尸体还烂在院子里。我们应该尽快处理。”

En: The rest is up to the boys in Processing. Maybe they will surprise us by doing their job for once, but I wouldn't count on it.
Zh: “剩下来的就交给分析部的那些小伙子了。也许他们这次能做好自己的工作，给我们个惊喜，不过我并没有抱太大指望。”

En: It would have been better if we could have pulled out the bullet and sent the body to Processing. But at least we got the body refrigerated.
Zh: “如果我们能把子弹取出来，把尸体送到分析部门就更好了。不过至少尸体已经被冰冻起来了。”

En: It would have been even better if we had been able to examine the bullet... but what really worries me is that the body is still decomposing in the yard.
Zh: “如果我们能够检查一下那枚子弹就更好了……不过真正让我担心的是那具尸体还烂在院子里。”

En: It's getting really painful for him, thinking about the body still being out there for people to see and children to desecrate.
Zh: 一想到尸体还挂在那里，任由人们看着，被小孩亵渎着……这真的让他很痛苦。

En: That's my forte. Unlike most cops, I understand how important *communication* is in our line of work.
Zh: “那是我的特长。不像大部分警察，我理解*沟通*对于我们这一行的重要性。”

En: I love doing interviews, and people love *me*. They love bathing in my glory.
Zh: “我喜欢走访，而且大家都喜欢*我*。他们喜欢沐浴在我的荣光之下。”

En: The *initial* interviews? Yes. Well, we talked to some people.
Zh: “*初步*走访？是的，呃，我们跟一些人聊过。”

En: I wish we didn't have to do any of the interviews. People are treacherous.
Zh: “希望我们不用做什么走访。那些人都奸诈的很。”

En: Very important indeed. Well, we conducted an interview with Evrart Claire.
Zh: “确实非常重要。呃，我们走访了艾弗拉特·克莱尔。”

En: I'm sure. Well, we conducted an interview with Evrart Claire.
Zh: “我确定。呃，我们走访了艾弗拉特·克莱尔。”

En: Mhm, including Evrart Claire. A daunting adversary if there ever was one.
Zh: “嗯，包括艾弗拉特·克莱尔。一个令人畏惧的对手。”

En: But, despite your misanthropy, we conducted an interview with Evrart Claire. No small task.
Zh: “但是，虽然你有厌男症，我们还是走访了艾弗拉特·克莱尔。这可不是个小任务。”

En: I'm glad we can agree on that, at least.
Zh: “很高兴我们至少能在这一点上达成共识。”

En: I hope your confidence will translate into results, officer.
Zh: “希望你的自信可以转换成结果，警官。”

En: He sighs. "One doesn't need to like the people one is interviewing. Indeed, it's better if you don't."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“你不用喜欢自己走访的人。当然了，如果你喜欢更好。”

En: Not always the right people, I'm afraid.
Zh: “恐怕不全是我们要找的人。”

En: Don't worry, Kim. I'll be in much better shape tomorrow.
Zh: “别担心，金。明天我的状态一定会好多。”

En: I look forward to that. As for the interviews...
Zh: “那我拭目以待了。至于走访的事……”

En: Don't worry, Kim. I'll totally be up for inspecting the body tomorrow.
Zh: “别担心，金。明天我一定会好好检查那具尸体。”

En: I look forward to that. As for the interviews...
Zh: “那我拭目以待了。至于走访的事……”

En: You've really moved up in his estimation.
Zh: 你在他的心中的评价确实提高了。

En: He's not saying much on the matter because he thinks you could have gotten more out of Evrart.
Zh: 他没有在这个问题上说太多，因为他觉得你能从艾弗拉特那里问出更多东西来。

En: I'm sure I can get him to tell us more.
Zh: “我肯定能让他说出更多消息。”

En: He's a tough cookie, that one.
Zh: “那个家伙，是个非常坚定的人。”

En: I'm a little intimidated by him, honestly. He has *stuff* on me.
Zh: “说实话，我有点害怕他。他手上有我的*把柄*。”

En: It was a matter of principle.
Zh: “这是原则问题。”

En: That was pretty unprofessional of me, I confess.
Zh: “我坦白，自己那样做真的很不专业。”

En: I'm not going to sit or stand or jump up in the air for some Union big wig just because!..
Zh: “我才不会为了什么工会的大人物坐下，站着或者跳到半空中呢，只是因为什么……”

En: Either way, we'll have to approach him again. Anyway... The lieutenant raises his eyebrows.
Zh: 警督抬起眉毛。“不管怎么说，我们都要再去找他……”

En: Say nothing. Just look at the city.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是看着这座城市。

En: Say nothing. Just look at the city.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是看着这座城市。

En: As for the interviews...
Zh: “至于走访的事……”

En: As for the interviews...
Zh: “至于走访的事……”

En: I just wanted to make sure he was really dead.
Zh: “我只想确认他真的死了。”

En: I still can't believe I missed that shot!
Zh: “我还是不相信自己居然打偏了！”

En: I admit, I'm a little out of practice.
Zh: “我承认，我是很久没有训练过了。”

En: He raises his eyebrows. "Right. Then we performed a field autopsy on the victim. We found some things we can really work with."
Zh: 他扬起眉头。“没错。然后我们对受害人进行了现场尸检。而且还发现了一些可以调查的线索。”

En: He raises his eyebrows. "Right. Then we performed a field autopsy on the victim. We didn't find as much as I'd hoped."
Zh: 他扬起眉头。“没错。然后我们对受害人进行了现场尸检。不过，我们发现的东西没有我期望的多。”

En: He raises his eyebrows. "Right. Then we performed a field autopsy on the victim. We didn't find as much as I'd hoped."
Zh: 他扬起眉头。“没错。然后我们对受害人进行了现场尸检。不过，我们发现的东西没有我期望的多。”

En: He raises his eyebrows. "Right. Then we performed a field autopsy on the victim. We didn't learn much, though."
Zh: 他抬起眉毛。“没错。然后我们对被害人进行了现场尸检。不过没找到什么有用的信息。”

En: He raises his eyebrows. "Right. We still have to perform the autopsy, though. But at least we did a thorough search of the crime scene."
Zh: 他扬起眉头。“没错。尸检还是要做的。不过至少我们对犯罪现场进行了详尽的搜查。”

En: He raises his eyebrows. "Right. We still have to perform the autopsy, though. And there's more work to be done at the crime scene."
Zh: 他抬起眉毛。“没错，不过我们还要进行尸检。而且犯罪现场还有很多工作等着我们呢。”

Zh: “不过我们还要进行尸检。但是，我们的确对犯罪现场进行了详尽的搜查。”

En: Being sober tends to help with precision. At any rate, we still have to perform the autopsy. And there's more work to do at the crime scene.
Zh: “清醒同样也有助于提高精准度。无论如何，我们还要做尸检，而且犯罪现场还有很多事要处理。”

En: Moving on to the interviews...
Zh: “继续说走访的事……”

En: However, you found that the hanged man wasn't just hanged -- he was also shot. That was some excellent detective work.
Zh: “但是，你发现那个男人不仅仅被吊起来了——还中枪了。这次勘查工作完成的*相当不错*。”

En: By *not ideal* he means he can't believe he let you try that trick and would be angry if he didn't feel the outcome was partly his responsibility.
Zh: 他说的那个*不理想*，意思是说他不相信自己居然真让你试了一下那个把戏，而且如果他不觉得这个后果也有自己一部分责任的话，一定会很生气的。

En: He studies you with mild concern. "Just don't get carried away. At any rate, your shot enabled us to perform a field autopsy on the victim. We found some things we can really work with."
Zh: 他带着一丝关心打量着你。“只是别得意忘形就好。总而言之，你那一枪让我们能对受害人进行现场尸检。而且还发现了一些可以调查的线索。”

En: He studies you with mild concern. "Just don't get carried away. At any rate, your shot enabled us to perform a field autopsy on the victim. We didn't find as much as I'd hoped."
Zh: 他带着一丝关心打量着你。“只是别得意忘形就好。总而言之，你那一枪让我们能对受害人进行现场尸检。不过，我们发现的东西没有我期望的多。”

En: He studies you with mild concern. "Just don't get carried away. At any rate, your shot enabled us to perform a field autopsy on the victim. We didn't learn much, though."
Zh: 他略带关切地打量着你。“只是不要太得意忘形就好。无论如何，你那一枪让我们能够对受害者进行现场尸检。不过，得到的信息不是很多。”
