En: In addition, we got that body *down* from the tree. *And* we performed a field autopsy on the victim. We found some things we can really work with.
Zh: “此外，我们把尸体从树上*放下来*了。*而且*还对受害人进行了尸检。我们还发现了一些可以调查的线索。”

En: In addition, we got that body *down* from the tree. *And* we performed a field autopsy on the victim. We didn't find as much as I'd hoped, though.
Zh: “此外，我们把尸体从树上*放下来*了。*而且*还对受害人进行了尸检。不过，我们发现的东西没有我期望的多。”

En: In addition, we got that body *down* from the tree. *And* we performed a field autopsy on the victim. We didn't find as much as I'd hoped, though.
Zh: “此外，我们把尸体从树上*放下来*了。*而且*还对受害人进行了尸检。不过，我们发现的东西没有我期望的多。”

En: In addition, we got that body *down* from the tree. *And* performed a field autopsy on the victim. We didn't learn much, though.
Zh: “另外，我们总算是把那具尸体从树上*弄下来*了。*而且*，对被害人进行了现场尸检。不过，得到的信息不是很多。”

En: In addition, we got that body *down* from the tree. We still have to perform the autopsy, though. But at least we did a thorough search of the crime scene -- that's great.
Zh: “此外，我们把尸体从树上*放下来*了。不过我们要进行尸检。但是至少我们对犯罪现场进行了详尽的搜查——这一点非常棒。”

En: In addition, we got that body *down* from the tree. We still have to perform the autopsy, though. And there's more work to be done at the crime scene.
Zh: “另外，我们总算是把那具尸体从树上*弄下来*了。不过还要进行尸检，犯罪现场也还有很多事要处理。”

En: Don't worry, Kim. I'll be in much better shape tomorrow.
Zh: “别担心，金。明天我的状态一定会好多。”

En: Though he wishes the body were out of the tree, he doesn't want you to feel too discouraged. Probably out of fear that you'll just give up and keep drinking...
Zh: 虽然他希望尸体已经不在树上了，但他不想让你太灰心。也许是担心你会直接放弃，然后继续酗酒……

En: As for the interviews...
Zh: “至于走访的事……”

En: Say nothing. Just look at the city.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是看着这座城市。

En: As for the interviews...
Zh: “至于走访的事……”

En: I look forward to that. As for the interviews...
Zh: “那我拭目以待了。至于走访的事……”

En: I look forward to that. As for the interviews...
Zh: “那我拭目以待了。至于走访的事……”

En: He looks at you with an unreadable expression. "The autopsy may reveal additional portents. As for the interviews..."
Zh: 他看着你，脸上带着难以辨认的表情。“验尸也许会揭示其他异常的地方。至于走访的事……”

En: I generally prefer to start with searching the scene and then move on to removing dead bodies.
Zh: “我一般更喜欢从搜查现场开始，然后再去处理尸体。”

En: I'm sorry I couldn't do more today. I'm just not a very good cop, am I.
Zh: “很抱歉我今天做的还不够多。我真的不是个好警察。”

En: Don't worry, Kim. I'll totally be up for getting the body out of the yard tomorrow.
Zh: “别担心，金。我明天一定会把尸体从院子里弄出去。”

En: With the end being so *nigh*, how can anyone expect me to focus on one corpse...
Zh: “世界末日都*快到*了，为什么还会有人期待我把注意力集中在一具尸体上面……”

En: Say nothing. Just look at the city.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是看着这座城市。

En: He looks at you with an unreadable expression. "The autopsy may reveal additional portents. As for the interviews..."
Zh: 他看着你，脸上带着难以辨认的表情。“如果这起凶杀案是一个前兆呢？至于走访的事……”

En: It's not my job to evaluate your methodology, officer. I'm just going through the facts. As for the interviews...
Zh: “我的工作不是评价你的方法论，警官。我只是在检查事实。至于走访的事……”

En: He sighs. "We're both doing our best under the circumstances. Just... don't drink tonight, please. As for the interviews..."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“在这种环境下，我们都在竭尽全力。只是……麻烦你今晚别喝酒了。至于走访的事……”

En: It's all part of the plan -- starting from the outside and working back toward the scene of the murder.
Zh: “这都是计划的一部分——从外面开始，然后回到凶案现场。”

En: He is not particularly satisfied with your progress, but he doesn't want you to feel completely discouraged. Probably out of fear that you'll just give up and keep drinking...
Zh: 他对你的进展不是特别满意，但也不想让你完全丧失信心。也许是担心你会直接放弃，然后继续酗酒……

En: He sighs. "We're both doing our best under the circumstances. Just... don't drink tonight, please. As for the interviews..."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“在这种环境下，我们都在竭尽全力。只是……麻烦你今晚别喝酒了。至于走访的事……”

En: It's not my job to evaluate your methodology, officer. I'm just going through the facts. As for the interviews...
Zh: “我的工作不是评价你的方法论，警官。我只是在检查事实。至于走访的事……”

En: I'm sorry I couldn't do more today. I'm just not a very good cop, am I.
Zh: “很抱歉我今天做的还不够多。我真的不是个好警察。”

En: With the end being so *nigh*, how can anyone expect me to focus on one corpse?
Zh: “世界末日都*快到*了，为什么还会有人期待我把注意力集中在一具尸体上面……”

En: We also performed a thorough search of the crime scene. All in all, we handled the situation very professionally. Now, for the interviews...
Zh: “我们还对犯罪现场进行了详尽的搜查。总而言之，我们非常专业地处理了这个情况。现在，说说走访情况……”

En: We also performed a thorough search of the crime scene. That's great. Now, for the interviews...
Zh: “我们还对犯罪现场进行了详尽的搜查。这一点非常棒。现在，说说走访情况……”

En: We did perform a thorough search of the crime scene, though. That's great. Now, for the *interviews...* He takes a deep drag and looks at the city.
Zh: “不过我们确实对犯罪现场进行了详细的搜查，这一点很好。现在，说到*走访*的事……”他深吸一口气，静静地看着这座城市。

En: There's still work to be done at the crime scene, however. We mustn't forget that. Now, for the interviews... He takes a deep drag and looks at the city.
Zh: “不过，犯罪现场还有些事没有处理完。这一点不能忘了。现在，说说走访情况……”他深深地吸了一口烟，看着这座城市。

En: There's still work to be done at the crime scene when we go back to deal with the body. We mustn't forget that. Now, for the interviews... He takes a deep drag and looks at the city.
Zh: “我们回去处理尸体的时候，还要留心一下犯罪现场，还有些事没有处理完。这一点不能忘了。现在，说说走访情况……”他深深地吸了一口烟，看着这座城市。

En: And there's still work to be done at the crime scene. Now, for the interviews... He takes a deep drag and looks at the city.
Zh: “而且犯罪现场还有很多工作要做。现在，说到走访的事……”他深吸一口气，静静地看着这座城市。

En: Well... He frowns. "We will have to work through that."
Zh: “呃……”他皱起眉头。“我们必须解决那个问题。”

En: Claire also helped you... remember your name, and is assisting you in getting your gun back. That's a relief.
Zh: “克莱尔还帮你……记起了你的名字，还在协助你寻找失枪。这还算是个安慰。”

En: Claire also helped you... how should I say? *Remember your name*? That's a relief.
Zh: “克莱尔还帮你……我应该怎么说？*记起你的名字*？我总算是松了一口气。”

En: I can't wait to get my gun back. I'll use that thieving Pigs for target practice, of course...
Zh: “我已经等不及要把枪找回来了。当然了，我还要用那个小偷猪猪女侠做射击训练……”

En: See, Kim. I'm becoming a real person.
Zh: “看见了吗，金，我变成一个真实的人了。”

En: I kind of miss being adrift, though -- nameless, gunless...
Zh: “不过我有点想念那种虚无缥缈的感觉——没有名字，没有枪……”

En: I don't know how I feel about my name, actually.
Zh: “其实，我不知道对自己名字有什么感觉。”

En: Big relief. It's a very serviceable name, too.
Zh: “大大的解脱。而且还是个非常有用的名字。”

En: I want a different name. One I haven't ruined yet.
Zh: “我想要个不同的名字。一个还没有被我毁掉的名字。”

En: You can look into the process of changing your name after we finish this investigation.
Zh: “我们的调查结束之后，你可以调查一下怎么改名。”

En: That's normal. It's best not to give it too much thought.
Zh: “这很正常。最好别想得太多。”

En: Kim must have had doubts about his name at some point too, but deliberately discarded them.
Zh: 金肯定也在某个时刻怀疑过自己的名字，但是又故意抛弃了这个想法。

En: Moving on...
Your personhood was never in doubt, officer. Moving on...
Zh: “我从来都没有怀疑过你的人格，警官。继续吧……”

En: I would advise against that, officer. Moving on...
Zh: “我劝你最好不要这样做，警官。继续吧……”

En: Although I see the appeal, being thus adrift interferes with performing one's duties. Moving on...
Zh: “虽然我能看出它的吸引力，不过虚无缥缈会影响你履行自己的义务。继续吧……”

En: I'm glad to have a lead on my gun, but I don't know how I feel about my name.
Zh: “我很高兴能找到枪的线索，但我说不出对自己的名字有什么感受。”

En: Have you ever wanted to change your name, Kim?
Zh: “你有没有想过换个名字，金？”

En: It's none of your business. Let it go.
Zh: 这跟你没有一点关系。放手吧。

En: Change? No, not exactly. But I think all of us at some point imagine what our lives might have been had we been something else.
Zh: “改名？没，不算是吧。但是我相信所有人在某个时刻都想象过，如果我们是另外一个人，生活又会是怎样一副模样。”

En: And then we feel trapped by the names we've been given, as symbols of the intentions and expectations of others... He pulls a long, pensive drag.
Zh: “然后我们会感到被自己的名字困住了，就好像是他人的意图和期望的象征……”他长长的，悲伤地叹了一口气。

En: Even if I were to change my name now, upon hearing any syllable that sounds like *kim* in the street, I'd turn to see who was calling me.
Zh: “就算我现在改名了，只要在街上听见一个类似*金*的音节，也会转头去看看是谁在喊我。”

En: But let's move on to the Wild Pines rep.
Zh: “不过，我们还是继续说说野松公司代表的事吧。”

En: But let's talk about Joyce Messier for a moment.
Zh: “不过，我们还是说说乔伊斯·梅西耶的事吧。”

En: Now that's a cool lady with a cool name.
Zh: “一个很酷的女士，拥有一个很酷的名字。”

En: I don't like her name. Or her, for that matter.
Zh: “我不喜欢她的名字。说到这一点，我也不喜欢她。”

En: We interviewed her and learned a lot.
Zh: “我们走访了她，得到了很多信息。”

En: Getting her to really talk to us took fantastic interpersonal skills and perseverance. Good work, detective.
Zh: “让她主动开口需要出色的人际交往能力和不屈不挠的毅力。你做的很好，警探。”

En: We tried to interview her, but she asked us to do something for her first.
Zh: “我们试过走访她，但她要求我们先为她做点事。”

En: I have a feeling Joyce knows how dangerous the situation really is. We *have* to get her to talk to us. He purses his lips.
Zh: 他咬紧嘴唇。“我有种感觉，乔伊斯知道现在的情况到底有多危险。我们*必须*让她开口。”

En: I have a feeling that she knows how dangerous the situation really is. We *have* to get her to talk to us. He frowns.
Zh: “我有种感觉，乔伊斯知道现在的情况到底有多危险。我们*必须*让她开口。”他皱紧眉头。

En: We talked to her, but didn't learn much...
Zh: “我们跟她谈过了，但得到的信息不多……”

En: We didn't get to that interview...
Zh: “我们还没去走访过……”

En: No, we didn't. It's very important that we do it tomorrow latest. He breathes out. A billow of silver smoke.
Zh: “不，我们没有。不过最迟明天一定要办完这件重要的事。”他呼出一口气，银色的烟雾寥寥升起。

En: If Kim is emphasizing something this much, it really must be important.
Zh: 如果金这么再三强调，那一定是很重要的事。

En: If Kim is emphasizing something this much, it really must be important. And dangerous. Very dangerous.
Zh: 如果金这么再三强调，那一定是很重要的事。而且很危险，非常危险。

En: I have a feeling Joyce knows how dangerous the situation really is. We *have* to get her to talk to us. He purses his lips.
Zh: 他咬紧嘴唇。“我有种感觉，乔伊斯知道现在的情况到底有多危险。我们*必须*让她开口。”

En: We tried to interview the Wild Pines rep, but she asked us to do something for her first -- but fine, he shrugs. "So be it."
Zh: “我们试着走访野松公司的代表，但是她要我们先为她办点事——不过没事，”他耸耸肩。“就这样办。”

En: We learned some interesting things along the way. We *have* to get Joyce to talk to us. I have a feeling she knows how dangerous the situation really is. But we also have to be careful about what we share with her.
Zh: “我们一路上了解到很多有意思的事。我们*必须*让乔伊斯开口。我有种感觉，乔伊斯知道现在的情况到底有多危险。不过我们跟她分享消息的时候，一定也要多加小心。”

En: We tried to interview her, but she asked us to do something for her first. Boy, did we find some bombshells along the way.
Zh: “我们试着走访过她，但是她要我们先为她办点事。伙计，我们这一路还是真是发现了不少让人震惊的事啊。”

En: Indeed. He pauses. "We *have* to get Messier to talk to us. I have a feeling she knows how dangerous the situation really is. But we also have to be careful about what we share with her."
Zh: “确实。”他停了一会儿。“我们*必须*让梅西耶开口。我有种感觉，乔伊斯知道现在的情况到底有多危险。不过我们跟她分享消息的时候，一定也要多加小心。”

En: Alright, but -- and, forgive me, this has been bothering me since this morning -- how have you managed to run around all day *wearing no shoes*?
Zh: “好吧，但是——还有，原谅我，这件事已经困扰我一上午了——你都*没穿鞋*怎么还能到处跑来跑去？”

En: Alright, but -- and, forgive me, this has been bothering me since this morning -- how have you managed to run around all day *wearing only one shoe*?
Zh: “好吧，但是——还有，原谅我，这件事已经困扰我一上午了——你*穿着一只鞋*怎么还能到处跑来跑去？”

En: It's part of my penance. I punish myself.
Zh: “这是我苦修的一部分，我在惩罚自己。”

En: I couldn't find my other shoe, and I refuse to wear shoes that aren't as cool as the shoe I'm wearing.
Zh: “我找不到另一只鞋了，而且我拒绝穿上没有现在脚上这只酷的鞋。”

En: I didn't even notice it. The work comes first, you know.
Zh: “我都没注意到。你也知道，我是工作第一的。”

En: Nice shoes, by the way. I like the green. Goes with the orange. He looks at your snakeskin shoes and smiles, suddenly.
Zh: 他看着你的蛇皮鞋，突然笑了。“话说回来，这些鞋倒是挺不错的。我喜欢这个绿色，跟橙色很配。”

En: I'm just that rugged.
Zh: “我就是这么的粗旷。”

En: It's part of my penance.
Zh: “这是我苦修的一部分。”

En: I really don't know. The investigation just seemed more important than looking for footwear.
Zh: “我真的不知道。调查感觉就是比找鞋穿更重要。”

En: Well, there seems to be a shoe that is a match to the one you're wearing further down the balcony. He points with his cigarette. "Perhaps you can collect it when we're done talking."
Zh: “呃，阳台上好像有只鞋跟你脚上这只是一对。”他用香烟指了指。“也许我们说完之后，你可以去把它捡回来。”

En: Well, there seems to be at least one shoe further down the balcony. He points with his cigarette. "Perhaps you can collect it when we're done talking."
Zh: “呃，至少阳台边那只鞋子看起来像是你的。”他用香烟指了指。“也许我们说完之后，你可以去把它捡回来。”

En: Imagine how much faster you could run wearing a cool patent leather shoe on *each* foot. He smiles suddenly.
Zh: 他突然笑了。“想象一下，如果你*每只*脚上都穿着一只酷炫的漆皮鞋，那该跑的多快啊。”

En: It's impressive, to say the least. For a man your age, especially... He nods thoughtfully, tapping his finger on his cigarette.
Zh: 他若有所思地点点头，用手指轻轻弹了下香烟。“退一步说，还挺让人印象深刻的。特别是你这个年纪的男人……”

En: Those are some stylish shoes too, by the way. Those loafers. Must be *hard* to run in those like you do. He smiles suddenly.
Zh: 他突然笑了。“话说，挺有型的，那双乐福鞋。像你这样穿着跑步应该挺*困难*的吧。”

En: It's impressive, to say the least. For a man your age... He nods thoughtfully, tapping his finger on his cigarette.
Zh: 他若有所思地点点头，用手指轻轻弹了下香烟。“退一步说，还挺让人印象深刻的。特别是你这个年纪的男人……”

En: But I guess those sneakers are a perfect fit for running around. Good buy, that. He smiles suddenly.
Zh: 他突然笑了。“不过我猜那双运动鞋很适合跑步。挺价廉物美的。”

En: The air outside is brisk, the lieutenant is silent for a moment. He listens to the traffic hum, then...
Zh: 外面的空气很清新，警督沉默了一会儿。他听着汽車的轰鸣声，然后……

En: Yes. The international community's mission in Revachol -- and the Moralintern more broadly.
Zh: “是的。国际社会在瑞瓦肖的使命——更宽泛的说，还有国际道德伦理委员会。”

En: What am I missing? What haven't I done?
Zh: “我还漏了什么？还有什么没所做的事？”

En: Ballistics. Let him cook here for a minute or two -- we can have another look at the set-up he had there, with the mattress. He nods southeast to the tower. "Ballistics always impresses these military types."
Zh: “弹道学。让他在这儿得瑟个一两分钟——我们再去他的定居点看看，有床垫的那个地方。”他朝着东南方向的小塔点点头。“弹道学总是能给那种军事咖留下深刻的印象。”

En: Remember, the lieutenant whispers. "He wants to tell us -- but he doesn't want to *help* us. It needs to look like we already have everything."
Zh: “记住，”警督低语到。“他想告诉我们——但他不想*帮*我们。要让他觉得我们已经知道一切了。”

En: We just need to get him back to the mainland. A crooked little smile. "We actually did it..."
Zh: “我们只要把他弄回内陆。”他露出一丝坏笑。“其实我们已经做到了……”

En: We really did, didn't we?
Zh: “我们已经做到了，不是吗？”

En: Let's get him back before we congratulate ourselves.
Zh: “在庆祝之前，最好先把他弄回去。”

En: He smiles. "You should be proud. Come on -- let's wrap this up."
Zh: 他笑了笑。“你应该感到骄傲。来吧——让我们结束这一切。”

En: He nods. "From nothing to this in a day..." A crooked little smile. "This is *good*, lieutenant-yefreitor, we just need a confession first, and then *maybe* a solid motive later."
Zh: 他点点头。“一天之内，从无到这个……”一丝扭曲的笑容。“这真的挺*好*的，荣誉警督。我们只要先拿到认罪口供，之后*也许*再来一个可靠的动机。”

En: He nods. "From nothing to this in a day..." A crooked little smile. "This is *good*, Detective Du Bois, we just need a confession first, and then *maybe* a solid motive later."
Zh: 他点点头。“一天之内，从无到这个……”一丝扭曲的笑容。“这真的挺*好*的，杜博阿警探。我们只要先拿到认罪口供，之后*也许*再来一个可靠的动机。”

En: He nods. "From nothing to this in a day..." A crooked little smile. "This is *good*, we just need a confession first, and then *maybe* a solid motive later."
Zh: 他点点头。“就在一天之内，从一无所有到现在……”他露出一丝狡黠的微笑。“这样*挺好的*，首先我们只需要一份认罪状，然后*也许*再来点可靠的动机。

En: Do you think he did it?
Zh: “你觉得是他做的吗？”

En: There is palpable excitement in his tone. He likes him for this -- *a lot*. But there's something more too...
Zh: 他的语调中透露出明显的兴奋。他喜欢这样——是*很喜欢*。但是还不止这些……

En: How do you think the questioning is going?
Zh: “你觉得询问进行的怎么样？”

En: What can I put on him -- to get him to fess up?
Zh: “我该怎么对付他——才能让他招供？”

En: I'm trying to get him to confess...
Zh: “我在尝试让他招供……”

En: Yes, good going -- that should be our *first* priority.
Zh: “是的，进展迅速——这应该是我们的*第一*优先级。”

En: Good. He nods. "We're doing very good. He *wants* to confess -- I can see that. We just need to pile it on, a little more -- the more we have on him, the closer he is..."
Zh: “很好。”他点点头。我们做的很不错。他*想要*招供——我能看出来。我们只要加点压力，再稍微多一点——我们加的越多，他就越接近……”

En: He's a communard. The victim was a mercenary. From Oranje. If everything else fails, get him talking politics... You could even tell him we already *have* a motive.
Zh: “他是个公社社员。受害人是一个雇佣兵。来自奥兰治。如果其他手段都失败了，就跟他聊政治……你甚至可以告诉他，我们已经*知道*动机了。”

En: He's a communard. The victim was a mercenary. We should get him talking politics again. You could even tell him we already *have* a motive.
Zh: “他是一个公社社员。被害人是个雇佣兵。我们应该再让他聊聊政治。你甚至可以告诉他，就说我们已经*找到*动机了。”

En: Okay, let's do this. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧，就这么做吧。”（总结。）

En: Remember, the lieutenant whispers. "He wants to tell us -- but he doesn't want to *help* us. It needs to look like we already have everything."
Zh: “记住，”警督低语到。“他想告诉我们——但他不想*帮*我们。要让他觉得我们已经知道一切了。”

En: Ballistics. Let him cook here for a minute or two -- we can have another look around the island for a sniper's nest. He looks to the small tower on the coast. "I think we should check out the post."
Zh: “弹道学。让他在这儿得瑟个一两分钟——我们再去岛上看看有没有狙击手掩体。”他看着海岸上的小塔。“我觉得应该去看看那个哨岗。”

En: It doesn't have to hold in court. Just establish a line of sight and he'll do the rest himself.
Zh: 没必要专门去法庭。只要构建出一条瞄准线，剩下的他自己会做完。

En: Also, the gun. He smiles very politely. "We should pick it up and discuss it in front of him. Make a show of it."
Zh: “还有，那把枪。”他礼貌的笑了。“我们应该把它捡起来，拿到他面前讨论。做一场秀。”

En: Yes, sire! Make a production of this examination!
Zh: 遵命，长官！来一场大张旗鼓的审问吧！

En: Well, I can think of one thing. The gun. He smiles very politely. "We should pick it up, and discuss it in front of him. Make a show of it."
Zh: “呃，我能想到一个东西。那把枪。”他礼貌的笑了。“我们应该把它捡起来，拿到他面前讨论。做一场秀。”

En: He thinks the deserter has more than just this case in him. Forty-four years here...
Zh: 他觉得这个逃兵身上不只背了现在这个案子。毕竟在这里已经44年……

En: You think he has more? Than this case?
Zh: “你觉得他还有问题？除了这个案子外？”

En: Pat him on the back. [Leave.]
Zh: 拍拍他的背。[离开。]

En: Oh yes... He nods. "And -- he hasn't spoken to anybody in a long time. If we get him open, who knows what will pour out..."
Zh: “哦，是的……”他点点头。还有——他已经很久没跟别人说过话了。如果我们能让他开口，谁知道能撬出些什么……

En: You hear that? He thinks you're *this* close...
Zh: 你听见了吗？他觉得你已经*这么*接近了……

En: I'm slapping myself to get ideas!
Zh: “我扇自己是为了获得灵感！”

En: I can see that, yes. I'm not judging.
Zh: “我能看见，没错。我不会说什么的。”

En: You think this will make them cooperate?
Zh: “你觉得这样就能让他们配合吗？”

En: Nothing will make them respect the RCM. But it will disrupt the game they prepared for us. We just stripped off one layer of whatever it is. Her decision to not corroborate their story was *definitely* not part of the plan.
Zh: “什么也不能让他们尊重RCM。但是可以扰乱他们为我们准备好的游戏。不论到底是什么，我们只是剥掉了其中一层。她决定不为他们的说法作证，*肯定*不是计划的一部分。”

En: Got it. Let's go. [Leave.]
Zh: “明白了。我们走吧。”[离开。]

En: (Say in a hushed voice.) "Why *did* she tell us all of that?"
Zh: （小声说道）“为什么她*要*告诉我们这一切？”

En: What else could she have done? Lie? She saw there was no way to lie and get away with it.
Zh: “她还能怎么做？撒谎？她发现没办法撒谎，也没办法彻底脱身了。”

En: You would have caught it.
Zh: 您本来可以捕捉到的。

En: (Nod.) "I would have caught it."
Zh: (点头。)“我本来可以捕捉到的。”

En: I'm not sure she *had* to lie. I wouldn't have known.
Zh: “我不确定她有撒谎的*必要*。我也不会知道。”

En: I have reason to doubt my ability to see through her lies.
Zh: “我有理由怀疑自己看穿她谎言的能力。”

En: If not you, then me. It was a smart move from her.
Zh: “如果你不知道，那我就更不知道了。她的这个举动很明智。”

En: Like hell you would have -- remember?
Zh: 比如你可能会——记起来吗？

En: She seems candid.
Zh: “她好像很坦白。”

En: (Whisper) "I know she's manipulative."
Zh: （低语）“我知道她很有手段。”

En: Something is off here.
Zh: “有点不太对劲。”

En: You think so? A shadow runs across his face. "Being candid is the best way to lie. The appearance of candour, with some facts thrown in, draws attention away from whatever one chooses to omit."
Zh: “你这么觉得吗？”他的脸掠过一片阴影。“坦白是最好的撒谎方式。率直的外表，加上一些事实，能把人们的注意力从他们选择遗漏的事情上转移开。”

En: She may be trying to control the pace of the investigation. Anyway, we should move. I suspect our inquiries will bring us back here soon enough.
Zh: “她可能想尝试控制调查的进展。不管怎么说，我们该走了。我想问询工作很快会把我们带回到这里。”

En: You think so? A shadow runs across his face. "She seemed forthcoming... Unusually so. Being forthcoming about some things is a good way to obscure other things."
Zh: “你这么觉得吗？”他的脸掠过一片阴影。“她看起来很愿意提供信息……一般都是这样。很乐意提供某些事的信息，是掩盖其他事的好办法。”

En: The best liars are always forthcoming... Anyway, we should move. I suspect our investigation will bring us back here soon enough.
Zh: “最好的骗子总是乐于提供信息的……不管怎么说，我们该走了。我想问询工作很快会把我们带回到这里。”

En: So you said to her.... What did you *mean* by it?
Zh: “所以你对她说……你说这话是*什么意思*？”

En: What do you mean by that?
Zh: “你这话是什么意思？”

En: (Whisper.) "She has half my skillset compromised, singing her tune. My assessments of her are untrustworthy."
Zh: （低语。）“她自己很有一套，对我一半的技能都有抵抗力。我对她的评价完全靠不住。”

En: She seemed forthcoming to me... Unusually so. Being forthcoming with some things is a good way to obscure other things. But I wouldn't call myself compromised...
Zh: “在我看来，她看起来很愿意提供信息……一般都是这样。很乐意提供某些事的信息，是掩盖其他事的好办法。但我自己也不是一个容易妥协的人……”

En: Perhaps that's because you *are*?
Zh: “也许是因为你*就是*这样的？”

En: Well. I am, anyway.
Zh: “呃，我是，无所谓了。”

En: I am not easily swayed by young women. But on the other hand... the best liars are candid. And she was candid.
Zh: “我不会轻易被年轻女人影响。不过话又说回来……最好的骗子都是很坦率的。而且她也很坦率。”

En: Anyway, we should move. I suspect our investigation will bring us back soon enough.
Zh: “不管怎么说，我们该走了。我想问询工作很快会把我们带回到这里。”

En: The lieutenant seems oddly sure he is not compromised, not psychologically at least. Is it hubris?
Zh: 警督对自己不会妥协这一点好像有种奇怪的执着，至少心理上没有。难道是因为傲慢吗？

En: Let's go get the flashlight then.
Zh: “那我们就去拿手电筒吧。”

En: The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。

En: No need to look for *supra-natural* explanations where a banal one will do. Now, let's go get the flashlight, shall we?
Zh: “没必要寻找什么*超自然*解释，用老一套的理由就够了。我们先去拿手电筒吧，怎么样？”

En: Now, let's move on, shall we?
Zh: “现在，我们该继续前进了，走吧？”

En: You didn't *have* to ram the door down, you know... He looks around. "No one's been here for ages."
Zh: “*没必要*把门撞开，你知道的……”他看了看这个“很久没人待过的地方”。

En: I doubt the electricity still works... Good thing we have a flashlight on us.
Zh: “不知道电还有没有用……还好我们身上带着手电筒。”

En: Looks like a gym to me.
Zh: “在我看来像是健身房。”

En: It's the netherworld. Beyond the veil.
Zh: “这是阴间。是来世。”

En: No idea, Kim... but my head hurts... my head hurts *bad*.
Zh: “不知道，金……但我的脑袋很疼……我的脑袋伤的*很重*。”

En: No, it's a gym, he disagrees. "Though it looks like no one's been here in ages..."
Zh: “不，这是个健身房，”他不同意这个看法。“不过看起来已经很久没人了……”

En: Yes, but no one's been here in ages. He draws a stripe on the dusty floor with his foot...
Zh: “是的，不过已经很久没人来过这里了。”他的脚在满是灰尘的地板上拖出一条痕迹……

En: We don't *have to* explore an abandoned gym, you know. Besides, no one's been here in ages...
Zh: “我们*没必要*探索一个废弃的健身房，你应该知道的。再说，已经很久没人来过这里了……”

En: It's an adventure!
Zh: “这是场冒险！”

En: Some specks of dust shimmer in a faint beam of daylight falling from the window. Other than that, the room is dark.
Zh: 窗户上落下些许灰尘，在日光下闪烁着点点光芒。除此之外，这个房间一片漆黑。

En: I doubt the electricity still works... We should find a flashlight if we want to go on. There should be one in my Kineema.
Zh: “不知道电还能不能用……如果想继续调查的话，应该找个手电筒。我的锐影里应该有一只。”

En: Some specks of dust shimmer in a ray of streetlight falling from the window. Other than that, the room is dark.
Zh: 窗户上落下些许灰尘，在街灯的照耀下闪烁着点点光芒。除此之外，这个房间一片漆黑。

En: I doubt the electricity still works... Good thing we have a flashlight on us. Don't forget to take it out of your bag before we move on.
Zh: “不知道电还能不能用……还好我们身上带着手电筒。走之前记得先从包里拿出来。”

En: An eerie feeling rises in your chest...
Zh: 一种奇异的感觉涌上你的心头……

En: What if there's a *reason* why no one's been here for ages...
Zh: “如果这里这么多年没人是有*原因*的呢……”

En: Let's just keep going, I'm sure it's just a regular abandoned house, nothing mysterious here.
Zh: “我们还是走吧，我肯定这里只是个普通的废弃房屋，没什么神秘的。”

En: Sounds good.
Zh: “听起来不错。”

En: Yes, because it's *closed*...
Zh: “是的，因为这里*关门*了……”

En: No need to look for *supra-natural* explanations where a banal one will do. Now, let's move on, shall we?
Zh: “没必要寻找什么*超自然*解释，用老一套的理由就够了。我们该走了吧？”

En: I think this may be the Artemitep's Boxing Club For Young Athletes.
Zh: “我觉得可能是针对青少年运动员的阿特米普拳击俱乐部。”

En: I think you're right, he says. "Though it looks like no one's been here in ages..."
Zh: “我觉得你说的对，”他说道。“不过看起来已经很久没人来过了……”

En: Now, let's go get the flashlight, shall we?
Zh: “我们去拿手电筒吧，怎么样？”

En: We can pick up where we left off tomorrow morning.
Zh: 警督点点头。“明天一早我们可以继续没完成的工作。”

En: I haven't paid the cafeteria manager for damages yet.
Zh: “我还没有向餐厅经理赔偿损失。”

En: But I don't have the money...
Zh: “但是我没钱……”

En: He's proud of you for getting your act together and finding a way to pay back what you owe.
Zh: 你终于振作起来，想办法还清了自己的债务，这让他为你感到骄傲。

En: Let's talk to him anyway. An officer of the RCM shouldn't be sleeping in the streets. We'll figure something out.
Zh: “不管怎么说，我们还是去跟他聊聊吧。RCM的警官不应该睡在大街上。我们会想出办法的。”

En: Though he finds this situation frustrating, he is doing his best to not make you feel any worse than you already do.
Zh: 虽然他发现眼下的状况让人有些沮丧，不过还是打算尽自己最大努力，让你的感觉不要比现在更糟糕。

En: Yes, my hostel room calls.
Zh: “是的，我的旅社房间在呼唤。”

En: I found lodgings in the village. I'll spend the night there.
Zh: “我在村庄里发现一些住所，打算去那里过夜。”

En: Good to know. I'll be wherever is necessary in the morning. Bright and early, 7:30 sharp.
Zh: 警督点点头。“很高兴知道这个。早上我会出现在那个地方。明天一大早，7:30分整。”

En: I'll also take a room at the Whirling.
Zh: “我也去飞旋旅社开个房间。”

En: I think I have the money now. This is money, right? (Show him reál bills.)
Zh: “我现在好像有钱了。这个是钱，没错吧？”（把雷亚尔钞票拿给他看。）

En: He nods. "I'll also take a room at the Whirling. One cannot get much closer to the crime scene."
Zh: 他点点头。“我也去飞旋旅社开个房间。离犯罪现场多近都不为过。”

En: You should take care of that, then.
Zh: “那你应该把这件事处理好。”

En: Let's continue tomorrow, bright and early -- 7:30 sharp. I'll be waiting downstairs.
Zh: 警督点点头。“我们明天再继续吧，就一大早——7：30分整。我会在楼下等你。”

En: I think I need to talk to the cafeteria manager about my room...
Zh: “我觉得应该去跟餐厅经理聊聊房间的事……”

En: You certainly have been avoiding it.
Zh: “你确实一直在逃避。”

En: Then I should check in with the cafeteria manager.
Zh: “那我应该去问问餐厅经理。”

En: Do that. I'll be waiting for you downstairs tomorrow.
Zh: 警督点点头。“去吧。明天我在楼下等你。”

En: Can't promise that. I might attack him again. [Leave.]
Zh: “这个没法保证。我可能会再袭击他一次。”[离开。]

En: The lieutenant groans, but doesn't say anything.
Zh: 警督叹息着，但终究没有说什么。

En: That's right, you *should* do it again! It's the *last* thing he'll be expecting.
Zh: 没错，你*应该*再做一次！这是他*最不愿意*见到的事。

En: Okay, I was trying to assert my dominance over him.
Zh: “好吧，我只是想维护我的权威而已。”

En: How else are we gonna get through the gates?
Zh: “不然我们怎么通过那些大门呢？”

En: But... I was fighting *bad*, unsavoury ideologies. I'm a hero.
Zh: “但是……我是在跟*坏的*、邪恶的意识形态作斗争。我是个英雄。”

En: We're not here on some political quest, we're here to solve a crime. Don't make this situation any more volatile than it already is.
Zh: “我们来这儿不是因为什么政治任务，我们是来破案的。不要把情况搞得比现在更复杂了。”

En: There must be some other way. Let's go see the yard again -- it faces the other end of the harbour. We're done with Measurehead here.
Zh: “肯定还有其他办法。我们再去院子里看看——它正对着港口的另一端。跟测颅先生已经说的差不多了。”

En: But *he's* the one trying to provoke *me*!
Zh: “但*他*才是想要*激怒*我的人！”

En: He nods. "Let's think of something else."
Zh: 他点点头。“我们再想想别的。”

En: I'm not even sure the *one bullet* my chamber holds would even prick that hulk.
Zh: “我甚至都不确定，我枪膛里的*一颗子弹*到底能不能穿透那个庞然大物。”

En: Alright, fine. I won't do it again. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧。我不会再这么做了。”[离开。]

En: No, no. You *should* do it again. It's the *last* thing he'll be expecting.
Zh: 不不。你*应该*再做一次。这是他*最不愿意*见到的事。

En: And you're just going to *let* him manipulate you like that? He raises an eyebrow.
Zh: “你就打算*让*他这样操纵你吗？”他扬起一边的眉头。

En: And the flashlight works a lot better if you *hold it in your hand*.
Zh: “而且，如果你把手电筒*握在手里*的话，可能会好很多。”

En: Fair point. Yes, totally obvious.
Zh: “说得好。没错，是很明显。”

En: This would be a lot easier with a flashlight...
Zh: “要是有个手电筒就容易多了……”

En: I've got one in the Kineema's toolbox. We should go back for it before we continue. No sense in tripping around in the darkness.
Zh: “我的锐影工具箱里应该有一只。我们应该先回去取，然后再继续前进。这样在黑暗里跌跌撞撞的毫无意义。”

En: Flashlights go *in* hand? (Stare at your hand uncomprehendingly.)
Zh: “把手电筒握在*你手里*？”（不解地盯着你的手。）

En: Yes, the lieutenant says. "You might even call it a *feature* of the universe, that you need to *hold* tools to *use* them."
Zh: “是的，”警督回答到。“你甚至可以称之为宇宙的一种*特征*，你需要把工具*握在手里*才能*使用*它们。”

En: Right. Now let's get to it, the lieutenant nods.
Zh: “好吧，现在让我们开始吧，”警督点点头。

En: That makes so much sense.
Zh: “这样就合理多了。”

En: That makes no sense at all.
Zh: “这样一点都说不通。”

En: I'm sorry the fundamental laws of the universe don't seem to agree with you, detective, but the fact remains that it's too dark to see in here without the flashlight...
Zh: 警督皱皱眉。“我很抱歉，宇宙的基本律法好像不太赞同你的意见，警探。不过事实没有改变，这里太黑了，如果没有手电筒，什么也看不见。”

En: So come on. We've got work to do.
Zh: “所以赶紧吧。我们还有工作要做。”

En: Got it, Kim, no need to rub it in. [Leave.]
Zh: “明白了，金，没必要反复说个没完。”[离开。]

En: Some areas are inaccessible without your FLASHLIGHT. After you’ve acquired it, go to your INVENTORY and equip it in a HELD slot to continue exploring.
Zh: 有些地方必须使用手电筒才可进入。你已经拿到手电筒了，可以在道具栏中装备它并继续探索。

En: Some areas are inaccessible without your FLASHLIGHT. After you’ve acquired it, go to your INVENTORY and equip it in a HELD slot to continue exploring.
Zh: 有些地方必须使用手电筒才可进入。你已经拿到手电筒了，可以在道具栏中装备它并继续探索。

En: He's right. This wasn't your grandma's game studio, this is something else.
Zh: 他是对的。这里不是你外婆的游戏工作室，是别的东西。

En: This is *way* above your tiny little policeman head.
Zh: 这个已经*严重*超越了你这个小警察的想象空间。

En: I don't know, I'm not an artist.
Zh: “我不知道，我不是艺术家。”

En: You're right, this isn't just a pen-and-paper role-playing game. They were trying to make it on *radiocomputers*.
Zh: “你说的对，这不仅仅是一个纸笔角色扮演游戏。他们想让游戏能在*无线电脑*上运行。”

En: Precisely! He agrees. "It's absolutely mad -- an *automated* role-playing game."
Zh: “就是这样！”他表示赞同。“他们绝对是疯了——一个*自动*的角色扮演游戏。”

En: He has respect -- and curiosity -- for this failed endeavour.
Zh: 他对这个失败的事业表示尊重——还有好奇。

En: Like he's ready to lay out a fine theory, crafted together like a puzzle box.
Zh: 就好像他已经准备好提出一个很好的理论，像拼图盒子一样拼在了一起。

En: Okay, well, *I* think... The lieutenant takes a step back, steepling his hands.
Zh: “好吧，这个嘛，*我*想想……”警督后退了一步，摩擦着双手。

En: And I don't use the *mad* lightly, he thinks.
Zh: 而且我用的这个*疯狂*可不轻，他这样想到。

En: Utter madness, he thinks -- as a compliment.
Zh: 彻头彻尾的疯狂，他想到——这是一种恭维。

En: His fascination has swept aside other concerns for the moment. He's a little *hooked*.
Zh: 这一刻，他的痴迷已经把其他关注点都抛在了一旁。他有点*上头*了。

En: Coordinating so many games would take a whole switchboard of people, possibly divided into sub-frequencies.
Zh: 协调这么多的游戏，那接线总机可能要很多人，也许被分成了一些子频段。

En: But I don't think *anyone* has attempted to create an *interisolary* game before. We just don't have the technology.
Zh: “但我不觉得之前曾经*有人*试过创造*跨洲*游戏。我们真的没有那种技术。”

En: *Not* in Revachol West, among the ruins.
Zh: *不会*出现在瑞瓦肖西部——这片废墟之间。

En: I don't think so. I think they genuinely believed... The lieutenant looks around the derelict room. The pipes howl and a rat crosses the floor.
Zh: “我不这么认为。我觉得他们由衷的相信自己能做到……”警督看了看这个荒废的房间。管道在低嚎，一只老鼠爬过地面。

En: And by 'these people' I mean people in Soona's radio game company -- Fortress Accident.
Zh: “我说的‘这些人’指的是苏娜那家无线游戏公司的人——要塞事故。”

En: Like he's ready to lay out a fine theory, crafted together like a puzzle box.
Zh: 就好像他已经准备好提出一个很好的理论，像拼图盒子一样拼在了一起。

En: It looks like one of those popular pen-and-paper role-playing games -- only these people were trying to *automate* it. Make it work on *radiocomputers*.
Zh: “看起来像是那种流行的纸笔角色扮演游戏——只不过那些人想试着让它变得*自动化*。让游戏能在*无线电脑*上运行。”

En: Utter madness, he thinks -- as a compliment.
Zh: 彻头彻尾的疯狂，他想到——这是一种恭维。

En: What do you think is going on here with the radiocomputer, chalkboard, and fireplace?
Zh: “你觉得这些无线电脑、黑板和壁炉是怎么回事？”

En: It's obvious -- this is what remains of Soona's radio game studio, Fortress Accident.
Zh: “很明显——这里是苏娜的无线游戏工作室‘要塞事故’的遗址。”

En: No time. Let's move. [Leave.]
Zh: “没时间了，我们走吧。”[离开。]

En: Yes, I got that. The lieutenant shifts on his feet. "What I meant was -- what were they trying to achieve with this damn game..."
Zh: “是啊，这个我明白。”警督换了只脚。“我的意思是——他们到底想用这个该死的游戏干什么……”

En: Looks like an undercover counterintelligence programme.
Zh: “看起来像是一个秘密的反间谍程序。”

En: Someone tried to exorcise *the curse* using technology?
Zh: “有人想用科学技术驱除*诅咒*吗？”

En: What were their ambitions? Because this here looks rather *advanced*.
Zh: “他们有什么野心？因为这里看起来很*先进*。”

En: It's just a failed business. The only question is -- what the hell were they making?
Zh: “只是个失败的生意罢了。唯一的问题是——他们到底在做什么鬼？”

En: Yes, that *is* the question... The lieutenant takes a step back, steepling his hands.
Zh: “是的，这*才是*问题……”警督后退了一步，摩挲着双手。

En: It looks like one of those popular pen-and-paper role-playing games -- only these people were trying to *automate* it. Make it work on *radiocomputers*.
Zh: “看起来像是那种流行的纸笔角色扮演游戏——只不过那些人想试着让它变得*自动化*。让游戏能在*无线电脑*上运行。”

En: No, that's not it. *I* think... The lieutenant takes a step back, steepling his hands.
Zh: “不，不是这样的。*我*觉得……”警督后退了一步，摩挲着双手。

En: Has anyone ever done this before?
Zh: “之前有人做过这个吗？”

En: How were they planning to do that?
Zh: “他们打算怎么做？”

En: Not to my knowledge. They make automated games in Graad, Messina, Königstein... You know, places with industry.
Zh: “据我所知没有。格拉德、墨西拿和柯尼希斯都在做自动化游戏……你知道的，那些工业发达的地方。”

En: Through call-in stations. He nods at the fireplace. "None of the players have to be physically present... Anyone in the world can participate in the game, as long as they have a two-way radio."
Zh: “通过电话站。”他朝壁炉点点头。“玩家不用本人在场……世界上的任何人都可以参加游戏，只要他们有双向无线电就可以了。”

En: And this was a role-playing game?
Zh: “这是个角色扮演游戏？”

En: Indeed. Those *welkins* are a dead giveaway. He points to the chalkboard. "Role-playing people love that stuff. The world looks like a modified version of the Wirrâl boardgame... with heat death thrown in."
Zh: “是的。那些*精灵*就是最明显的证据。”他指向黑板。“玩角色扮演的人喜欢这类东西。世界看起来像是威勒尔桌游的改良版……只是里面加入了热寂（猜想宇宙终极命运的一种假说）。”

En: What do you think happened to the company?
Zh: “你觉得公司发生了什么事？”

En: No idea... They stopped filling out the schedule on the chalkboard.
Zh: “不知道……他们停止在黑板上填写计划表了。”

En: Got you.
Zh: “明白了。”

En: Super cool! Someone should give them millions of reál *immediately*. This game is too good to be left unfinished.
Zh: 太酷了。某个狂拽酷炫屌炸天的人应该*立即*给他们投资个几百万的雷亚尔。这个游戏太棒了，不能就这么半途而废。

En: Wow. (Conclude.)
Zh: “哇哦。”（总结。）

En: Indeed. It's ambitious. And *untethered* from reality, but... The lieutenant tilts his head, thinking.
Zh: “确实，野心勃勃。而且没有被现实*束缚*，但是……”警督歪着头，思考着。

En: Then there's the Game Master Frequency that listens in on the smaller call-in stations. I think that was supposed to coordinate the stories. Functioning as a master of ceremonies of sorts.
Zh: “然后有一个游戏主频率，会监听比较小的电话站。我觉得那个应该是配合故事的，功能类似主持人之类的吧。”

En: Do we have any *money*? Let's give them more money so they can finish it and make it even bigger.
Zh: “我们身上有*钱*吗？我们多给他们一点钱，这样他们就能把游戏做得更大了。”

En: They were insane if they thought they could do this.
Zh: “如果他们觉得自己能做到，那就真的是疯了。”

En: It was just a play to cheat money out of their investor's pockets.
Zh: “这只是一场从投资人口袋里骗钱的游戏。”

En: A half-smile breaks out on his face. "It's too late for that, I'm afraid," he says, looking around the derelict room. The pipes howl and a rat crosses the floor.
Zh: 他的脸上露出勉强的笑容。“恐怕现在已经太晚了，”他看了看这个荒废的房间说道。管道在低嚎，一只老鼠爬过地面。

En: The curse got them, I see no other explanation.
Zh: “他们中诅咒了，我看不到别的解释。”

En: Okay, he concludes. "Let's keep moving."
Zh: “好吧，”他总结到。“我们继续行动吧。”

En: The world is cold and lonely. This would keep it company. Let's *finish* it.
Zh: “这个世界冰冷又孤寂。这个能与它作伴。让我们*完成*它吧。”

En: Yes -- especially in here. The lieutenant looks around the derelict room. The pipes howl and a rat crosses the floor in front of your feet.
Zh: “是的——特别是这里。”警督看了看这个荒废的房间。管道在低嚎，地板上有只老鼠爬过你的脚前。

En: Ah yes, the *doom* of bad business practices... The lieutenant looks around the derelict room. The pipes howl and a rat crosses the floor.
Zh: “啊，是的，糟糕商务实践的*宿命*……”警督看了看这个荒废的房间。管道在低嚎，一只老鼠爬过地面。

En: Maybe this is all her plan? She might have heard about you.
Zh: 也许这全都是她的计划？她也许听说过你。

En: How did you hope it would go?
Zh: “你希望怎么样？”

En: Honestly, I was expecting you to use your *unorthodox technique* to keep her off-balance -- and you know... *not* volunteer us to be her henchmen.
Zh: “说实话，我希望你用你的*非传统技术*让她失去平衡——还有，你懂得……*不要*自愿做她的跟班……”

En: Oh, so we're henchmen now?
Zh: “哦，所以现在我们是跟班了？”

En: Her *proposal* was likely part of her design. This all is.
Zh: “她的*提议*很可能是她设计的一部分。这些全部都是。”

En: The company must have more ears on the ground -- she could have known about my *condition*.
Zh: “公司在这里肯定有耳目——她可能已经知道我的*状况*了。”

En: This woman is running circles around us. She might have known about your *misplaced badge* all along -- or she's simply an adept improviser. Either way, we've played straight into her hands.
Zh: “这个女人在跟我们兜圈子。她也许早就已经知道你*丢失的警徽*——或者她只是在见机行事。不管怎么说，我们都是正中她的下怀。”

En: I wouldn't go that far. Her reaction did come off as sincere to me... I think she's just quick to adapt. She is a professional, after all.
Zh: “我不会想那么远。她的反应在我看来的确算是真诚的……我觉得她只是很快就适应了。毕竟，她可是专业的。”

En: Yes. Knowing this does not really change our position, however.
Zh: “是的。不过知道这个也没有改变我们的立场。”

En: What do you propose -- that we *don't* investigate the drug trafficking?
Zh: “你有什么建议——我们*不要*调查毒品走私吗？”

En: Let's get back to her then. [Conclude.]
Zh: “那我们回去找她吧。”[总结。]

En: No. If there is reasonable suspicion, we must investigate. Otherwise she could claim we're siding with the Union. Or that we're on their take. We'd never hear the end of it...
Zh: “不。如果有合理的怀疑，我们就必须调查。不然她可以宣称我们跟工会是站在一边的，或者是我们受贿了。这样就永远听不到最后……”

En: What I propose is -- we ask her. Then we investigate. *Briefly*... He adjusts his glasses. "But do *not* share the outcome of this investigation with her. We tell her it's done and demand for her information on the lynching."
Zh: “我的提议是——去问她。然后调查。*简略些*……”他调整了一下眼镜。“但是*不要*跟她分享调查结果。我们就跟她说调查已经结束，需要她提供关于私刑的信息。”

En: We could just, you know, find my badge.
Zh: “我们可以，你知道的，找到我的警徽。”

En: Oh, he nods slowly. "That would be *fantastic*. But do we have the time? The world is large -- and your badge is eight-by-six centimetres."
Zh: “哦，”他慢慢点点头。“那就真是*太好了*。但是我们有那个时间吗？这个世界这么大——而你的警徽只有8x6厘米。”

En: The situation might have changed drastically by the time you locate it. Time is of the essence...
Zh: 等你找到它的时候，情况很可能已经彻底改变了。时间才是至关重要的……

En: You could request a new one from your station but that would literally take *months*.
Zh: “你可以要求警局给你发个新的，不过那样可能真的会花上*好几个月*。”

En: He doesn't let it show, but there is a limit to how much the consequences of your unprofessional behaviour can cost the investigation.
Zh: 他没有表现出来，但是你那不专业行为带来的后果只能让调查付出有限的代价。

En: I am sorry for putting us in this situation -- I'll handle it.
Zh: “抱歉，是我让我们俩陷入这种境地的——我会处理好的。”

En: I'm sure you will, detective.
Zh: “我确定你可以的，警探。”

En: It would not hurt if you *tried* more in the future, yes.
Zh: “不过如果你未来再多*努力*一点，也没什么大不了的，是这样没错。”

En: This isn't going very well.
Zh: “事情进展的不太顺利啊。”

En: Really? I thought it was going so well.
Zh: “真的吗？我觉得挺顺利的啊。”

En: And the... sort of... quivering jello thing with the eye? (Point to your twitching eye.)
Zh: “而且……还有点……眼皮底抖得像果冻一样？”（指着你发炎的眼睛。）

En: Is it, though? 'Nothing'? You're still in for a bumpy ride here, try not to mess it up.
Zh: 不过，真是这样吗？‘没什么大不了’？你还会遇到困难的，试着别搞砸了。

En: What was what?
Zh: “什么怎么了？”

En: It was nothing.
Zh: “没什么。”

En: You mean the sweating and the fidgeting when he mentioned her name?
Zh: “你是说当他提到她名字的时候，那副汗流满面，坐立不安的样子？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: It's an art thing. You wouldn't understand.
Zh: “这是艺术。你不会理解的。”

En: Try me.
Zh: “说来听听。”

En: 'Tis art.
Zh: 是艺术。

En: The victim? Is there something I should know before we talk to her?
Zh: “被害人？在我们跟她交谈之前，有什么我应该知道的吗？”

En: I think I know that woman.
Zh: “我觉得我认得那个女人。”

En: It's not nothing, you were coming apart at the seams. He looks you in the eye: "Do you know the assault victim?"
Zh: “没什么，你差点崩溃了。”他看着你的眼睛：“你认识那个受害者吗？”

En: You looked strange when he mentioned the assault victim's name. He looks you in the eye: "Do you know that woman?"
Zh: “当你提到那个受害者名字的时候，看起来有些奇怪。”他看着你的眼睛：“你认识那个女人吗？”

En: He's not leaving you a lot of room to back off from this.
Zh: 他没有给你留下退让的空间。

En: Yes.
Zh: “认识。”

En: No.
Zh: “不认识。”

En: He repeats: "Officer, before we interview this woman -- is there something I should know?"
Zh: 他重复到：“警官，我们调查这个女人之前——有没有什么我应该知道的？”

En: Is there something I should know before we talk to her?
Zh: “在我们跟她交谈之前，有什么我应该知道的吗？”

En: I met her. In the hallway, after I woke up.
Zh: “我见过她。在我醒来之后，在走廊里见过。”

En: She knows I didn't remember being a cop.
Zh: “她知道我不记得自己是警察了。”

En: I tried to hit on her...
Zh: “我试过和她搭讪……”

En: She knows I did some pretty crazy stuff before I passed out. That I partied hard. And not very mental-healthily.
Zh: “她知道我在晕过去之前做了很疯狂的事情。狂欢的有点过头。而且还知道我精神状况不太好。”

En: I asked her where we are, what city is this, maybe even *what year*. Something like that.
Zh: “我问过她我们在哪，这座城市叫什么，也许甚至问过现在是*什么年代*。等等之类的事情吧。”

En: Nothing -- let's move on. [Leave.]
Zh: “没什么——我们继续吧。”[离开。]

En: That's it. [Leave.]
Zh: “就这样吧。”[离开。]

En: I didn't rape her.
Zh: “我没有强暴她。”

En: What you're saying is... he says in a low, calm voice. "*You* raped her?"
Zh: “你的意思是……”他的声音变得低沉又镇静。“*你*强暴她了吗？”

En: Ice is forming on the inside of your chest cavity, cold sweat trickles down your neck...
Zh: 你感觉自己的胸腔内部结起了坚冰，冷汗顺着你的脖子往下淌……

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: Then don't *ever* say that again. There's a pause.
Zh: “那就*不要*再提这个了。”他停顿了一下。

En: Understood -- you were not in good shape on Monday.
Zh: “明白——你周一状况不太好。”

En: What else?
Zh: “还有什么吗？”

En: Whatever you do, don't say the first thing.
Zh: 不管做什么，第一条不能说。

En: Okay, he nods. "That's manageable."
Zh: “好吧，”他点点头。“还能应付。”

En: It *is*, he nods. "This would have been good to know *before*."
Zh: “*是的*，”他点头。如果能*早点*知道就好了”

En: It didn't come up.
Zh: “没找到机会。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Anything else?
Zh: “还有别的吗？”

En: It should have. Anything else?
Zh: “本来应该发生的。还有别的吗？”

En: So she overheard you? Or did you *party* together?
Zh: “所以她是无意中听到你的？或者你们一起*派对*了？”

En: So this is what it feels like to be *interviewed*...
Zh: 原来这就是被*走访*的感觉……

En: Not very fun.
Zh: 不是很有意思。

En: I got nothing to hide.
Zh: 我没什么好隐瞒的。

En: Fun.
Zh: 有意思。

En: I wouldn't say it is.
Zh: 我不这么认为。

En: What if you do -- but you just don't *remember* that you do?
Zh: 如果你确实做了——但你只是*不记得*了？

En: You'll get through it.
Zh: 你会熬过去的。

En: You'll get through it, you slippery fuck! Blast some insane-o at him, throw him off.
Zh: 你会熬过去的，你这个狡猾的混蛋！对他爆粗口，把他扔出去。

En: Of course it is, you slippery fuck! Blast some insane-o at him, throw him off.
Zh: 当然是了，你这个狡猾的混蛋！对他爆粗口，把他扔出去。

En: She overheard me listening to music and doing weird things. From her room. It's the one next to yours.
Zh: “她偷听到我在听音乐，还有做些奇怪的事——从她的房间。就是你旁边那间。”

En: Yeah, we partied all right. Partied all night like the Inzany Boys.
Zh: “是啊，我们玩了一整晚。像野小子一样嗨到天亮。”

En: I'm not going to answer that, lieutenant.
Zh: “我不会回答这个问题的，警督。”

En: I'm going to assume she overheard you -- anything else?
Zh: “我假设她偷听了你——还有别的吗？”

En: Wonderful. I'm going to assume she overheard you -- anything else?
Zh: “很好。我就假设她偷听了你——还有别的吗？”

En: Anything else?
Zh: “还有别的吗？”

En: He shrugs. "That's clearly your interrogation technique. Anything else?"
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“那明显是你的审讯技巧。还有别的吗？”

En: This is unprofessional. He repeats: "Not sharing information with me is *unprofessional*."
Zh: “这一点也不专业。”他重复到：“不跟我分享信息这一点很*不专业*。”

En: We'll be all right, lieutenant. This is nothing.
Zh: “我们会没事的，警督。这不算什么。”

En: He nods. "We'll be all right, officer. This is nothing."
Zh: 他点点头。“我们会没事的，警官。这没什么大不了的。”

En: His opinion of you has lowered considerably.
Zh: 他对你的看法大大降低了。

En: You feel fortified by his assurance. It's going to be all right.
Zh: 他的担保让你感到坚强。一切都会没事的。

En: Understood. He's stone-faced.
Zh: “明白。”他毫无表情。

En: It went pretty okay, I guess.
Zh: “我猜应该挺好的吧。”

En: She said I'm going to have to interrogate her later and it's going to *suck* for me.
Zh: “她说我稍后肯定会审问她，对我来说一定会*很糟糕*。”

En: Better not to add anything to that.
Zh: “最好不要继续深入这个话题了。”

En: It doesn't matter how it *went* officer -- what else?
Zh: “*进行*的怎么样一点也不重要——还有什么吗？”

En: Mhm, he nods. "What else did you do?"
Zh: “嗯，”他点点头。“你还做了什么？”

En: This means it's well funded. Technology like that... a major player must be financing it.
Zh: 这就意味着资金十分充足。像那样的技术……肯定有个重量级人物在为它提供资金。

En: Listening in on your calls? Between you and your station? A worrying prospect...
Zh: 窃听你的通话？你跟你们分局之间的吗？这种前景让人担忧……

En: The probe and the case *converged*, he thinks. This was quite the find.
Zh: 调查和案件*合而为一*，他想到。这的确是个不得了的发现。

En: He just doesn't like flimsy connections. Especially of *this* kind.
Zh: 他只是不喜欢薄弱的联系。特别是*这一类*的。

En: Actually, he's not bad at it at all.
Zh: 其实，他做得一点都不差。

En: Yes, well... He doesn't say more.
Zh: “是的，这个嘛……”他不再言语。

En: A lot of women there. Especially for a lady driver's cabin.
Zh: “那里有很多女人。特别是对于一个女司机的驾驶舱来说。”

En: Maybe the trader is some sort of cinephile.
Zh: “也许交易商是某种影迷。”

En: Could the film industry be involved?
Zh: “也许电影业也参合了一脚？”

En: I'm going to say -- no.
Zh: “要我说的话——没有。”

En: Unimportant?
Zh: 不重要？

En: In short: this is a future angle. Possibly a good one.
Zh: 总之：这是未来的方向，而且可能很不错。

En: Meaning: someone as slippery as Evrart won't be caught by a couple of cops and some stuff they found in some cabin.
Zh: 意思是：像艾弗拉特那种狡猾的泥鳅是不会被一群警察和他们在乘坐舱里找到的一点东西抓住的。

En: Will the RCM open an investigation into this?
Zh: “RCM会展开调查吗？”

En: We should return to the murder case. See what Joyce tells us about the lynching. When we're done for the day, I'll call my station and suggest our narcotics department look into it.
Zh: “我们应该回到凶杀案上。看看乔伊斯对私刑有什么可以说的。等今天的工作结束之后，我给警局去个电话，建议我们的缉毒部门调查一下。”

En: There are more than enough grounds to start an official investigation -- some time later, when we're done here. We do *not* want to get caught in that... He stops to think.
Zh: “已经有充足的理由可以展开正式调查了——等我们这里完事之后。我们可*不想*被困在那里……”他停下来想了一会儿。

En: What are you thinking?
Zh: “你在想什么？”

En: The fact that one hasn't started already gives me pause. An investigation, I mean. Especially if the Madre grouping is involved... and I can't imagine they aren't. It's certainly worrisome.
Zh: “还没有展开任何调查的事实就已经引起我的深思了。我的意思是调查。特别是如果马德雷集团也牵扯其中的话……我无法想象他们没有。这的确很让人担心。”

En: Corruption?
Zh: “腐败吗？”

En: He nods. "All the same, I don't like the idea of Internal Affairs descending on the matter. That won't help anyone either."
Zh: 他点点头。“我还是不喜欢让内务部介入调查的想法。这样帮不了任何人。”

En: And last but not least, it's *Ruby's cabin* we found. This is an undeniable connection to the Union.
Zh: “还有最后重要的一点是，我们发现的是*露比的車厢*。这与工会之间存在不可否认的关联。”

En: The Union really is in neck-deep with everything around here, isn't it?
Zh: “工会真是深陷于周围的一切，不是吗？”

En: One way or another, they seem to be -- logistically, if nothing else. But don't expect to bust this open during our stay here. At best, this is an angle we can use against them. To other ends. As extra ammunition.
Zh: “以这样那样的方式吧，他们似乎是这样没错——如果没有别的，从逻辑上讲是的。不过我们在这里停留的期间，不用指望能查出些什么。最多也就是我们用来对付他们的一个角度。作为额外附加的把柄。”

En: Seems like something police would do. What do you think of all this, Kim?
Zh: “看起来像是警察该做的事。你觉得这一切是怎么回事，金？”

En: I don't think there's anything to discuss. [Leave.]
Zh: “我觉得没什么好讨论的。”[离开。]

En: The lieutenant nods -- but he's not happy.
Zh: 警督点点头——但他并不高兴。

En: I'm not sure what the ULAN frequencies are all about, but they may hold some significance. Perhaps it's a better way to connect between fleets while avoiding frequency bleed, or maybe it's used to tap into RCM networks...
Zh: “我不确定ULAN频率是怎么一回事，但是说不定很重要。也许这是車队之间更好的联系方式，同时还能避免频繁的流血事件，或者它以前可能是接入RCM网络的……”

En: Oh, and the maps we found. They reveal the *geographical* extent of the operation -- looks like they've used abandoned tunnels and access roads to stay hidden. This is useful info.
Zh: “哦，还有我们发现的地图。它们揭示了经营活动的*地理*范围——看来他们使用了废弃的隧道和通道来保持隐蔽。这是很有用的信息。”

En: How do you think this is connected to the Union?
Zh: “你觉得这跟工会有什么关联？”

En: My suggestion is -- we use it against the Union in any way we can. To our own ends. It's a slippery eel, but we just might be able to pin them down *indirectly*. Down the road.
Zh: “我的建议是——我们尽可能地利用它来对抗工会。来达到我们的目的。这是一条滑溜溜的鳗鱼，不过也许我们可以*间接地*压制住他们……在将来的某个时候。”

En: What about the movie posters? How do they factor into all this?
Zh: “那电影海报呢？它们又有什么关联？”

En: As... *elegant* as they are, I don't think they are relevant to the drug trade.
Zh: “尽管……它们很*优雅*，我还是不觉得跟毒品交易有关。”

En: I'm especially intrigued by that radio transmitter -- particularly the sheer number of stations it can connect. Looks like this alleged drug trade casts a wide net.
Zh: “我对那个无线电发报机特别好奇——特别是它连接了那么多电台。看来这个所谓的毒品交易真是广泛撒网啊。”

En: Honestly, I'm quite worried by what we've seen so far. The evidence seems to point to a rather extensive and well-organized operation.
Zh: “说实话，从我们目前的发现来看，我真的有点担心。证据似乎指向了一个特别庞大而又组织有序的经营活动。”

En: So it turns out this *is* is connected to the Union.
Zh: “所以*确实*跟工会有关联。”

En: Okay -- debrief over? [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧——汇总完毕？”[离开。]

En: We didn't find anything *conclusive* linking them to the smuggling operation, but somehow I doubt that Evrart Claire would be oblivious to something like this happening right under his nose.
Zh: “我们没有找到任何*决定性*的证据，能把他们跟走私行动联系起来，但是我有点怀疑艾弗拉特·克莱尔会完全无视这些在他鼻子底下发生的事。”

En: Like Joyce told us, yes -- logistically. But don't expect to bust this open during our stay here. At best, this is an angle we can use against them. To other ends. As extra ammunition.
Zh: “就像乔伊斯告诉我们的——逻辑上是这样没错。但是我们待在这里的时候，不要指望能打破这个缺口。充其量只是我们拿来对他们的一个角度……来达到其他目的。作为额外的弹药。”

En: Don't be fooled, desire always plays a role.
Zh: 别被糊弄了，欲望总是能发挥作用的。

En: Klaasje might have been right about Ruby -- she looks awfully lot like one of the poster girls in the cabin.
Zh: “卡拉洁对露比的看法也许是对的——她看起来确实跟車厢海报上的女孩十分相似。”

En: And last but not least, it looks like the Hardie boys knew this driver, as we know that she was present at the lynching. This may be the Union connection we've been searching for.
Zh: “还有最后一件——但并不是最不重要的事，看来哈迪兄弟认识这个司机，我们也知道私刑发生的时候她在场。这也许这就是我们要找的工会的联系。”

En: Perhaps you're right... He stops to think. "This is one connection I find a little dubious, to be honest. I prefer not to profile people's emotions. Perhaps it's because I'm not very good at it, but still."
Zh: “也许你是对的……”他停下来想了想。“老实说，这个联系让我感觉有些可疑。我宁愿不去分析别人的情绪。也许是因为我不太擅长吧，不过还是不愿意。”

En: Debrief over, he nods. "After you."
Zh: “汇总结束，”他点点头。“你先走。”

En: Eclectic taste? This shop is full of *stolen* goods -- a blight on the community!
Zh: 不拘一格的风格？这家商店到处都是*偷来的*商品——对社区造成了很坏的影响！

En: You misunderstood me. I think it's super cool and *gangster* that he sells stolen goods.
Zh: “你误解我了。我觉得他贩卖偷来的东西其实挺酷的，而且很有*帮派范儿*。”

En: I wouldn't go that far but... He shrugs. "Why not?"
Zh: “我倒不会说那么过，但是……”他耸耸肩。“为什么不呢。”

En: How so?
Zh: “怎么会这样呢？”

En: Won't make my fortune here, but it does seem like a good place to start.
Zh: “这里应该不会让我发大财，但确实像是个开始的好地方。”

En: I'm pretty sure you can pick up some odds and ends to sell here -- besides my spinners... He nods toward a table of knick-knacks. "The owner seems to have *eclectic* taste."
Zh: “我很肯定你能找到些零零碎碎的东西拿到这里卖——除了我的轮圈之外……”他朝着那一桌子的小摆设点点头。“主人的风格好像有些*不拘一格*。”

En: I'm pretty sure you can pick up some odds and ends to sell here. He nods toward a table of knick-knacks. "The owner seems to have *eclectic* taste."
Zh: “我很肯定你能找到些零零碎碎的东西拿到这里卖。”他朝着那一桌子的小摆设点点头。“主人的风格好像有些*不拘一格*。”

En: *Or* we could shut this place down? The stuff is clearly stolen.
Zh: “*或者*我们可以把这里封了？这些东西明显是偷来的。”

En: On the contrary -- we should do our part to *support* the local economy.
Zh: “正好相反——我们可以尽自己的一份力量，*支持*当地经济。”

En: (Lower your voice.) "You do realize this place is full of stolen goods, right?"
Zh: （压低你的声音）“你知道到这里的东西都是偷来的，对吧？”

En: We can't go around punishing the people we're supposed to protect for breaking laws they don't even know exist.
Zh: “我们不可能到处惩罚那些本来应该保护的人，仅仅是因为他们违反了那些在他们眼里根本不存在的法律。”

En: Oh... Yes, I see what you're getting at. I guess we should just let it slide -- and maybe even profit?
Zh: “哦……没错，我明白你的意思。我猜我们应该顺其自然——甚至是让他赚点钱？”

En: Shouldn't we at least ask where some of these things came from?
Zh: “我们不是至少应该问问这些东西是从哪里来的吗？”

En: True. There are inherent injustices in the social system I -- and all of us -- find ourselves in.
Zh: “没错。社会制度中存在着固有的不公，我——还有我们所有人——都身处其中。”

En: He nods. "Go ahead, do your part to support this grass-roots initiative."
Zh: 他点点头。“来吧，尽你所能，支持一下这位基层发明家。”

En: It *would* help our investigation if you weren't so hard up for cash.
Zh: “如果你不是那么缺钱的话，*可能*对我们的调查更有用些。”

En: It won't help our investigation to antagonize people we could interview.
Zh: “对于我们的调查来说，激怒那些可以走访的人并没有什么帮助。”

En: It *would* help our investigation, he adds, his voice softening as he looks off to the side, "if you weren't so hard up for cash."
Zh: “这样*可能*对我们的调查有所帮助，”他补充到，当他看向周围，声音也变的柔和起来。“如果你没有这么缺钱的话。”

En: It pains him to see a fellow officer struggling to make ends meet and not be able to offer assistance. This is his way of making it up to you.
Zh: 看到警察同事挣扎求生，自己还无法提供援助，这让他感到很痛苦。这是他补偿你的一种方式。

En: ...we shouldn't disrupt the way things are done here, he thinks. And have little power to do so anyway.
Zh: ……我们不应该破坏这里的做事方式，他想到。再说了，也没有什么权力这么样。

En: My financial situation is none of your business.
Zh: “我的经济状况与你无关。”

En: I've accepted my lot -- sleeping on park benches, getting by on scraps...
Zh: “我已经接受了这一切了——睡在公园的长凳上，靠些残羹冷炙过活……”

En: My financial situation is great! Superstars don't have problems with finances!
Zh: “我的经济状况好的不得了！超级明星根本不会遇到财务问题！”

En: I am fully deserving of the trials life has dealt me.
Zh: “生活给我的一切考验，都是我自己应得的。”

En: Perhaps, but it's no use punishing yourself more.
Zh: “也许，但是继续这样惩罚自己也没有用。”

En: He looks at you seriously. "An officer of the Citizens Militia can't live like that. We won't be able to complete *this investigation* if you live like that."
Zh: 他严肃地看着你。“公民武装的警官不能这样生活。如果你继续这样下去，我们就没办法完成*这次调查*了。”

En: The lieutenant's tone doesn't betray any frustration. Concern -- yes.
Zh: 警督的口吻没有流露出任何的失望。担心——是有的。

En: He's not too comfortable counselling you like this, but he's genuinely concerned for your well-being.
Zh: 这样给你做心理咨询让他感到不太舒服，但他真心很担心你的健康。

En: It's not my intention to make it my business, just to move this investigation forward.
Zh: “我本来也不想多管闲事，只是推进调查而已。”

En: He is mildly offended that his display of camaraderie went wrong -- though it doesn't show in his posture.
Zh: 表达友情的方式出了岔子，这让他感到有些生气——虽然他的姿势并没有显露分毫。

En: Sure. But one's financial situation could always be even better, right?
Zh: “当然。但是一个人的经济状况总是可以改善的，对吧？”

En: He doesn't really think you're a superstar or that you're well-off. He's only playing along because it's expedient.
Zh: 他不是真以为你是个超级明星或者说你很富裕。他卖关子是因为这样才是权宜之计。

En: I'm pretty sure you can pick up some odds and ends to sell here -- besides my spinners... He nods toward a table of knick-knacks. "The owner seems to have *eclectic* taste."
Zh: “我很肯定你能找到些零零碎碎的东西拿到这里卖——除了我的轮圈之外……”他朝着那一桌子的小摆设点点头。“主人的风格好像有些*不拘一格*。”

En: I'm pretty sure you can pick up some odds and ends to sell here. Take a look around. He nods toward a table heaped with knick-knacks. "The owner has eclectic taste."
Zh: “我很肯定你能找到些零零碎碎的东西拿到这里卖。在周围看看吧。”他朝着那一桌子的小摆设点点头。“主人的风格好像有些不拘一格。”

En: Mhm. I plan to *enterprise* my way out of this jam. I have a vision.
Zh: “嗯。我打算*开公司*来摆脱这个困境。我脑子里已经有愿景了。”

En: Sure. Why not? It doesn't sound like he believes in the *vision*.
Zh: “当然可以。为什么不呢。”听起来他不是很相信这个*愿景*。

En: Oh, this? (Point to your face.)
Zh: “这个？”（指着你的脸。）

En: What happened to you? the lieutenant asks as he leans in, eyes mapping your frozen face.
Zh: “你怎么了？”警督打量着你冻僵的脸，倾过身子问道。

En: What do you mean, lieutenant?
Zh: “你是什么意思，警督？”

En: I am Icebreaker.
Zh: “我是破冰者。”

En: No, you are not. What you are is an officer of the Revachol Citizens Militia conducting a murder investigation.
Zh: “不，你不是。你是瑞瓦肖公民武装的一名警官，正在调查一件谋杀案。”

En: This is what I am now, Kim. What I was always meant to be.
Zh: “我现在就是这样，金。我一直都是这样。”

En: I plough my way towards the frigid lands of Katla and Vaasa, smashing every ice sheet in my path...
Zh: “我艰难地向着卡特拉和瓦萨城的寒冷之地前行，粉碎了前路上的每一片冰原……”

En: No, but you see, you *don't*, the lieutenant explains patiently, although his voice is very tired suddenly. "You're not a firewalker, you're not an icebreaker."
Zh: “不，好好看看，你*不是*，”警督耐心地解释着，但是声音却突然变得很疲惫。“你不是火行者，也不是破冰者。”

En: No, but you see, you don't, the lieutenant explains patiently, although his voice is suddenly very tired. "You're not an icebreaker or a firewalker, or even Tequila Sunset."
Zh: “不，你知道你*不是*，”警督耐心地解释着，但是声音却突然变得很疲惫。“你既不是破冰者，也不是火行者，更不是龙舌兰日落。”

En: Nothing, *nothing* can stop the Icebreaker...
Zh: “没有什么，*没有什么*能阻止破冰者……”

En: Are you fucking insane?! the lieutenant snaps at you. "Don't answer that! And STOP doing it! Let's go."
Zh: “你疯了吗？！”警督厉声对你说道。“不用回答！也别这样了！我们走吧。”

En: No, but you see, you don't, the lieutenant explains patiently, although his voice is suddenly very tired. "You're not an icebreaker or a firewalker, or even Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau."
Zh: “不，你知道你不是，”警督耐心地解释着，但是声音却突然变得很疲惫。“你既不是破冰者，也不是火行者，更不是拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托。”

En: No, but you see, you don't, the lieutenant explains patiently, his voice suddenly tired. "You don't plough anything, you help me solve this case, please remember."
Zh: “不，你知道你不是，”警督耐心地解释着，但是声音却突然变得很疲惫。“你不用艰难前行，你只需要帮我破这个案子就行了，请你记住这一点。”

En: No, but you see, you don't, the lieutenant explains patiently, but his voice is very tired. "Your name is Harrier Du Bois, not 'Icebreaker'."
Zh: “不，好好看看，你不是，”警督耐心地解释着，但是声音却突然变得很疲惫。“你的名字是哈里尔·杜博阿，不是‘破冰者’。”

En: No, but you see, you don't, the lieutenant explains patiently, but his voice is very tired. "You're not an icebreaker, Harry. Please stop."
Zh: “不，你知道你不是，”警督耐心地解释着，但是声音却突然变得很疲惫。“你不是破冰者，哈里。打住吧。”

En: No, but you see, you don't, the lieutenant explains patiently, but his voice is very tired. "You are not Tequila Sunset and you are not an icebreaker. Stop, please!"
Zh: “不，你知道你不是，”警督耐心地解释着，但是声音却突然变得很疲惫。“你不是龙舌兰日落，也不是破冰者。打住吧！”

En: No, but you see, you don't, the lieutenant explains patiently, but his voice is very tired. "You're not Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau and you're not an icebreaker!"
Zh: “不，你知道你不是，”警督耐心地解释着，但是声音却突然变得很疲惫。“你不是拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托，你也不是破冰者！”

En: I'm sorry, Kim, I don't know what came over me.
Zh: “抱歉，金，我不知道自己是怎么了。”

En: It's okay, he says quietly. "We're both under a lot of stress right now. Let's just try to not let it get to us and remember what we came here to do."
Zh: “没事，”他平静地说道。“我们现在承受的压力都很大。但是我们尽量别让压力影响到我们，要时刻记得我们是来做什么的。”

En: It is a powerful metaphor, lieutenant. For my resolve.
Zh: “这个比喻对我来说很恰当啊，警督。”

En: Okay, that's all right. He looks visibly relieved. "As long as you understand it is a metaphor and don't build a persona around it."
Zh: “行，那就好。”他似乎是松了口气。“只要你明白这只是比喻就好了，千万别因为这个产生新的人格。”

En: They say you can hear the ice crack from a...
Zh: “他们说你能听到冰块破裂的声音……”

En: I understand. It's not gonna become a thing.
Zh: “我明白，这不是什么大不了的事情。”

En: I understand *nothing*.
Zh: “我*什么*也没理解。”

En: Oh, good. The lieutenant's posture relaxes. "Thank you! And sorry I asked about it. It's none of my business. Let's just get back to work."
Zh: “哦，很好。”警督放松了下来。“真是谢谢你了！很抱歉问了这种问题。这本来不关我的事。我们还是回去工作吧。”

En: You look... different. Constipated somehow?
Zh: “你看起来有些……不一样了。便秘了吗？”

En: Suddenly the whole Icebreaker concept seems... just lame.
Zh: 突然间，破冰者这个说法似乎变得……很蹩脚。

En: It's like he re-conditioned your brain.
Zh: 就好像是他重新调整了你的大脑。

En: The lieutenant's command instantly lets all the wind out of your sails, like he overrode your mind or flicked some secret master-switch.
Zh: 警督的命令让你立刻泄了气，仿佛他控制了你的思想，或是打开了你的某个秘密开关。

En: Yes, your face. He nods, looking worried. "Looks like you're having a stroke. Can you even move it?"
Zh: “没错，你的脸。”他有些担忧地点点头。“你看起来像中风了一样。还能动吗？”

En: Better keep the lieutenant in the dark about the whole 'Icebreaker' thing. He's not gonna get it.
Zh: 最好不要和警督说起破冰者的事情。他是不会明白的。

En: There's nothing more to talk about, only to *endure*. [Leave.]
Zh: “没什么可说的，只要*忍耐*就好。[离开。]

En: That's a negative, lieutenant.
Zh: 这就是拒绝，警督。

En: And what is that? A walking mask?
Zh: “那是什么？一只会走路的面具？”

En: A noble sufferer.
Zh: “一名高贵的受害者。”

En: Absolutely. (Lift the corners of your mouth with index fingers.) "See?"
Zh: “当然。”（用食指戳戳嘴角。）“瞧见没？”

En: I don't need to 'move it', this is my final form.
Zh: “我不需要‘动’，这是我的最终形态。”

En: He Who Stares Straight into the Eclipse.
Zh: “他是凝视日食之人。”

En: He Who Withstands All Temptation.
Zh: “他是能抵挡一切诱惑之人。”

En: The last kingsman.
Zh: “最后的王下骑士。”

En: I'm fine, don't worry about it.
Zh: “我很好，不用担心。”

En: So now you want to go blind as well? He shakes his head. "How about we become Those Who Solve Murder Cases instead and get back to work?"
Zh: “你也想成瞎子吗？”他摇着头。“我们不如成为侦破凶杀案之人，回去工作吧？”

En: Including drinking?
Zh: “包括喝酒吗？”

En: Excluding drinking.
Zh: “除了喝酒。”

En: Including drinking.
Zh: “包括喝酒。”

En: Excluding drinking. And drugs.
Zh: “除了喝酒，还有嗑药。”

En: No, you are not. What you are is an RCM lieutenant conducting a murder investigation.
Zh: “不，你不是。你是一名RCM警督，你正在调查一起谋杀案。”

En: You don't look fine and I'm *very* worried about it. He crosses his arms on his chest.
Zh: “你看起来不太好，我*很*担心你。”他把手臂交叉在胸前。

En: I just don't understand, officer. The lieutenant just stands there, looking perplexed. "Please, help me understand."
Zh: “我真是不太明白，警官。”警督困惑地站在那儿。“请帮我解释一下。”

En: Should I contact his station's lazareth? Behind his back?
Zh: 我要不要背着他联系他警局的*检疫所*？

En: I don't think *you* are supposed to call yourself that. A fleeting smile crosses his lips. "But I appreciate the honesty. Now let's get back to work."
Zh: “我觉得*你*不应该那样称呼自己。”他的唇边扬起一抹微笑。“但感谢你说了实话。好了，我们回去工作吧。”

En: Neither was an actual question.
Zh: 这都不是什么实际的问题。

En: The lieutenant is *not* loving the new look.
Zh: 警督*不*喜欢新造型。

En: The last kingsman... he repeats pensively, inspecting your face. "That's fitting, I suppose. Almost as if you *did* manage to turn back time. If only for yourself."
Zh: “最后的王下骑士……”他看着你的脸，沉闷地重复道。“如果你*真的*让时间倒流了，我觉得倒是挺恰当。哪怕那么做是为了自己。”

En: Alright, detective, he reaches out and pats you on the arm. It feels strangely encouraging. "Let's get going, we've got a killer to catch."
Zh: “好了，警探，”他伸出手，拍拍你的胳膊。你莫名有一种被鼓舞的感觉。“我们继续工作吧，还有凶手等着我们去抓呢。”

En: Face of a millipede trapped in amber...
Zh: 被困在琥珀中的千足虫的脸……

En: But somehow it doesn't scare him, like it scared you. Doesn't make him quiver with cold. Poor guy...
Zh: 可这并没有吓到他，反而像是把你吓了一跳。而且也没让他冷得发抖。可怜鬼……

En: What temptations is *He* withstanding then? asks the lieutenant with a puzzled look.
Zh: “那*他*在抵挡什么诱惑呢？”警督疑惑地问道。

En: You know what? I don't even want to know. Let it be one of your *internal* side-cases. I just want to get back to work.
Zh: “告诉你吧，我根本不想知道答案。留给你自己的*内心*去解决吧。我只想回去工作。”

En: Hm. The lieutenant seems almost impressed. "Admitting a problem is the first step forward. Perhaps now we can finally get some work done."
Zh: “嗯。”警督似乎有些满意。“承认错误是前进的第一步。现在我们应该终于能工作了。”

En: Shall we? He looks ready to go.
Zh: “我们走吧？”他似乎准备离开了。

En: Wait, am I becoming some sort of Death's Head Racist suddenly?!
Zh: “等等，我突然就变成某种骷髅头的种族主义者了吗？”

En: I don't know what you mean by *death's head* but it certainly looks like you're becoming a racist, officer.
Zh: “我不知道你说的*骷髅头*是什么意思，但是看起来你确实变成了一个种族主义者，警官。”

En: We know a *lie*, sire. And it didn't feel like one to us. He chooses to believe though -- for now.
Zh: 吾辈知道什么是*谎言*，大人。而这个看起来并不是。但是目前——他选择相信了。

En: And one more thing -- as you've seen, these sorts of attitudes are quite common in Martinaise. So I expect this to happen again. And again...
Zh: “还有一件事——你也看到了，这种看法在马丁内斯很常见，所以我觉得这种事以后还会一次又一次的发生……”

En: I've had this conversation enough times in my life. From now on, I'm  going to stay out of it. How you choose to respond is your business. He concludes: "But be careful. Now, let's move on."
Zh: “我的一生当中，已经听过很多次这样的话了。从今以后，我会置身事外。你选择怎样的回应是你的事。”他总结到：“不过还是要小心点。现在，我们继续工作吧。”

En: Okay... feels like this is going to be a *discussion* now.
Zh: 好吧……现在感觉这会变成一场*辩论*。

En: Okay. You think there's a way out of this.
Zh: 好吧。好吧。你以为总会有办法的。

En: Relax, I was practising the time-honoured technique known as the 'lie'.
Zh: “放松，我在练习一种由来已久的技术，它的名字叫做‘撒谎’。”

En: It's basic biology.
Zh: “这是基础生物学。”

En: Look, what happens when the races get all mixed up?
Zh: “看，种族全部混在一起会发生什么？”

En: I'm going to *stop* this conversation. He makes a sudden hand gesture. An open palm. "This is Martinaise. People have certain attitudes here. You will find plenty of citizens to agree with you. As for me..."
Zh: “我要*终止*这次谈话了。”他突然比了个手势，摊开的手掌心。“这里是马丁内斯。这里的人是秉持着某种特定看法的。的确会有很多市民同意你的观点。至于我……”

En: (Shrug.) "Let Nature sort them out."
Zh: （耸肩。）“让大自然来解决吧。”

En: I've got nothing against them, but we have a responsibility to future generations.
Zh: “我对他们没有任何意见，但是我们必须为后代负责。”

En: Well, he says after taking a moment to collect himself. "You've made your views on the subject quite clear..." Another little pause.
Zh: “好吧，”他稍微调整了一下自己，然后开口说道，“你对这个问题的看法已经很清楚了……”又是一次短暂的停顿。

En: I suppose that's a relief. Just be careful how often you avail yourself of this 'technique'. A lie may be useful in the short-term, but they have a way of catching up with you later.
Zh: “这样也算是种安慰。只不过要小心你自己使用这种‘技术’的频率。短期来看，谎言也许有用，但是之后，它们总有办法惩罚你。”

En: I was just trying to convey that I understood his grievances and wasn't judging him for having them.
Zh: “我只是想试着传达一个观点：我理解他的抱怨，而且也不会因此去评判他什么的。”

En: Everyone thinks they're better than other people. Some people are just particularly crude about it.
Zh: “每个人都觉得自己比别人好。有些人对此特别粗鲁。”

En: The lieutenant falls silent and looks down at his boots. Whether he thinks you have a point or not, he isn't saying. It's unclear what this means for your relationship. Then he speaks...
Zh: 警督陷入沉默，低下头看着自己的鞋子。不论他认为你的话有没有道理，他都没有说明。这对你们的关系来说意味着什么，谁也不清楚。之后，他开了口……

En: Yes.
Zh: 是的。

En: Dresscode: RCM official.
Zh: 着装要求：RCM警官。

En: Dresscode: Royalist reenactor.
Zh: 着装要求：保皇派重演者。

En: You're right. I didn't mean to cause trouble. Sorry about that.
Zh: “你是对的。我不想制造麻烦。很抱歉。”

En: That's good to hear. I don't want there to be any unnecessary friction in our relationship during this *important* case.
Zh: “听你这么说我很高兴。我不希望在这么*重要*的案件调查期间，你我之间的关系产生什么不必要的摩擦。”

En: Here we go. Zoom in on the mixed races part. It's the best one.
Zh: 来吧。聚焦在种族混合的部分，这是最好的。

En: I don't really want to talk about it. [Leave.]
Zh: “我真的不想谈论这个。”[离开。]

En: The lieutenant looks at you in silence, though you cannot discern if he's judging you or not. "I suppose your reasons are your own."
Zh: 警督一言不发地看着你，虽然你也辨别不出他是不是在评判你。“我觉得你的理由只属于你自己。”

En: And what exactly do you think should happen to those with *undesirable* traits?
Zh: “那你觉得拥有那些*不良*特征的人应该怎么样呢？”

En: Now you're thinking sensibly.
Zh: 现在你是在理性地思考。

En: Well, now you've done it. Let's see if you can talk your way out of this one.
Zh: 好吧，现在你做到了。让我们看看你能不能用自己的方式解决这个问题。

En: The lieutenant narrows his gaze. "So you *don't* find his views... say, *unbefitting of a police officer*?"
Zh: 警督眯起双眼。“所以你*不觉得*他的观点……怎么说呢，*不太适合一个警察吗？*”

En: It's not about me. As police officers, we gain nothing by staking out the moral high ground over him.
Zh: “这与我无关。作为一个警察，就算站在他的道德制高点之上，我们也得不到什么。”

En: Of course. But he's also not entirely responsible for them. I haven't been in this world for long -- already I'm a product of my environment.
Zh: “当然。但是他也不是完全需要对他们负责。我在这个世界待得不长——而我已经是周边环境的产物了。”

En: There are tens of thousands of people who grew up in Martinaise and somehow managed to avoid becoming racists who yell 'Come here, saffron picker' at passers by.
Zh: “有成千上万的人在马丁内斯长大，但却没有让自己变成一个种族主义者，不会对过往的路人叫喊什么‘过来啊，摘番红花的’。”

En: Our position in Revachol is already precarious: Many people think we're vigilantes as it is. I can't have them thinking we're *racist* vigilantes to boot.
Zh: “我们在瑞瓦肖的地位已经很危险了：很多人都觉得我们是义务警员。我可不能让他们觉得我们是*种族主义*的义务警员。”

En: Well, *actually*...
Apology accepted, detective. The lieutenant relaxes. A weight seems to have been lifted from his shoulders.
Zh: “我接受你的道歉，警探。”警督松了一口气，肩膀上好像卸下了沉重的负担。

En: Wait a second. Are you sure you want to go there? Even if you succeed, you're going to fail.
Zh: 等一下。你确定想这样做吗？即使成功，你也会失败的。

En: What do you mean? I'm in the right here.
Zh: 你什么意思？我不就在这里吗。

En: On second thought, maybe I don't.
Zh: 再仔细一想，也许还是算了。

En: Are you sure about that?
Zh: 你确定吗？

En: Forget it, this was a dumb idea.
Zh: 算了吧，这主意太蠢了。

En: Hoo boy, we're really doing this.
Zh: 好家伙，我们真要这么做了。

En: Maybe he's got a point.
Zh: “也许他说的有道理。”

En: And what point would that be?
Zh: “那是什么意思呢？”

En: There's a difference between saying something crude -- and an ideological racist.
Zh: “说话粗鲁——跟思想上的种族主义者，两者是有区别的。”

En: The lieutenant says nothing, waiting for you to continue.
Zh: 警督什么也没说，等着你继续。

En: It's only rational for a society to select for the most desirable traits. That's how it works in Nature.
Zh: “对于一个社会来说，选择最可取的特征是很合理的。自然界就是这么运作的。”

En: That doesn't mean they don't have rights. If they're perpetrators, then we punish, if they're victims, then we help. That's all there is.
Zh: “那也不代表他们没有权利。如果他们是犯罪者，我们惩罚，如果是受害者，我们帮助。就是这个样子。”

En: Wonderful. The lieutenant falls silent. His expression is hard to read, but it certainly doesn't look *happy*. Then he speaks...
Zh: “很好。”警督陷入沉默。他的表情很难解读，但看起来肯定不*高兴*。然后他开口了……

En: Let me say just one more thing. As you've seen, these sorts of attitudes are quite common in Martinaise. So I expect this to happen again. And again...
Zh: “我再说一件事。你也看到了，这种看法在马丁内斯很常见，所以我觉得这种事以后还会一次又一次的发生……”

En: Stop right there. I know what you're going to say. Don't. Also -- these attitudes are quite common in Martinaise. So I expect this to happen again. And again.
Zh: “好了，打住。我知道你要说什么。别说。还有——这种看法在马丁内斯很常见，所以我觉得这种事以后还会一次又一次的发生……”

En: I've had this conversation enough times in my life. From now on, I'm going to stay out of it. How you choose to respond is your business. He concludes: "Now -- let's get back to the task at hand."
Zh: “我的一生当中，已经听过很多次这样的话了。从今以后，我会置身事外。你选择怎样的回应是你的事。”他总结到：“现在——让我们继续回到手头的工作吧。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Finally. I thought I have to come looking for you. What took you so long?
Zh: “你终于回来了。我都准备去找你了。你怎么耽误那么久？”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Just men being men, Kim. You wouldn't understand. (Look solemnly to the horizon.)
Zh: “我也是个普通人啊，金。你是不会明白的。”（严肃地望向地平线。）

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: We got into some pretty deep waters there.
Zh: “我们聊的蛮深入的。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: It was *unbelievably* manly.
Zh: “真是*太*有男人味了。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Right... He nods. "Did he teach you to turn back time?"
Zh: “的确……”他点点头。“他教过你怎么让时间倒流吗？”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: The sarcasm is so finely tuned it actually comes off like worry.
Zh: 这讽刺如此完美，甚至听起来像是在担忧。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: We became one.
Zh: “我们合为一体了。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Really? What happened?
Zh: “是吗？发生什么了？”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Probably shouldn't have asked you this.
Zh: 也许不应该问你这个问题。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Time flies when you're in a good company -- how long was I in there?
Zh: “要是有好的同伴，就会感觉时间过得很快——我在那儿待了多久？”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Couple of months, a year maybe? he replies, glancing at his wrist-watch. "What happened in there?"
Zh: “几个月，也可能是一年？”他瞥了一眼手表，回答道。“那边怎么了？”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: René! he says, snapping his fingers.
Zh: “雷内！”他扳着手指说道。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: As if a revelation just hit him.
Zh: 仿佛刚刚获得了启示一般。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: What about René?
Zh: “雷内怎么了？”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: You think he's our *killer* after all?
Zh: “你觉得他是我们要找的*凶手*？”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Just ignore him.
Zh: 不理会他。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: No, I don't. But you are reminding me of him more and more. It's not a compliment, I don't like it.
Zh: “那倒不是。不过你越来越让我想起他了。这不是在夸你，因为我不喜欢这样。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: You are really starting to remind me of him. It's not a compliment. I don't like it.
Zh: “你让我想起他的事情来了。这不是在夸你，因为我不喜欢这样。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: You're not going to like it.
Zh: 你不喜欢这样。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: What happens in Measurehead's mother's office stays in Measurehead's mother's office.
Zh: “在测颅先生母亲的办公室里发生的事情，就永远只能留在那个房间。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: No, but he did seem to believe it's possible.
Zh: “不，但他好像觉得有这个可能。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: No, he thought it's a bad idea and that I should stop obsessing about it.
Zh: “不，他觉得那样不好，说我不该继续纠结。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: That's probably best for everyone. He nods. "Let's go then."
Zh: “可能这样最好吧。”他点点头。“我们走吧。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: No, he didn't even want to share his novel techniques...
Zh: “不，他甚至都不想分享自己的新奇技巧……”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: That's probably best for everyone. A smirk flashes over his face as he turns to move. "My condolences of course, but solving this case will make you feel better. I promise."
Zh: “可能这样最好吧。”他转身走开，脸上浮现出一抹笑容。“我当然很同情你，不过我保证，把这个案子解决了一定能让你好受些。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Huh... The lieutenant thoughtfully rubs his chin as he turns to move. "So there is something we agree on after all. Didn't see it coming. But let's move, I'm getting cold."
Zh: “嗯……”警督若有所思地揉了揉下巴，转身走开。“原来我们还是能达成共识的啊。真是没想到。走吧，我有点冷了。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Based on other things he *believes* I wouldn't put much weight on it. He turns to move. "Now let's get back to work."
Zh: “他*以为*的事情多了去了，我可不会当回事。”他转身走开。“我们回去工作吧。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: I don't need a word-for-word retelling, but we're partners -- say *something*. An adjective will do.
Zh: 我不想一字一句复述一遍了，但我们可是搭档——说点*人话*吧。哪怕是形容词都行。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: His serious, business-like tone doesn't waiver for a second.
Zh: 他这样严肃又公事公办的语气丝毫没有消退。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Right... He nods, covering his mouth with the back of the hand. "Did he teach you a way to turn back time?"
Zh: “对……”他点点头，用手背挡住嘴。“他教过你怎么让时间倒流吗？”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Trying to hide a smile.
Zh: 他在尽力掩饰笑容。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Wasn't that back when you became his 'race pupil'?
Zh: “那是你还在当他的‘种族学生’时的事了吧？”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: That was about race. *This* is about sex. I admire him.
Zh: “当时是种族，*这回*是性。我很敬佩他。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: No. That was different.
Zh: “不，那不一样。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: I hope you don't take offence if I don't pressure you for details. We really need to get back to work. He smiles.
Zh: “如果我不想继续追问细节，希望你别觉得被冒犯了。我们真的得回去工作了。”他笑道。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: I was like a blind horse when he *led* me to water and made me drink.
Zh: “他*带着*我去水边喝水时，我觉得自己就像一匹瞎了眼的马。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: That's alright, Kim. Let's move. [Leave.]
Zh: “没事，金。我们走吧。”[离开。]

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Good good, that's very good. The smile persists as he turns to leave. "I almost feel bad that we need to focus on our investigation now. But we do. Let's go."
Zh: “很好，非常好。”他转身离开时脸上仍然挂着笑容。“我现在真是不想继续调查了。可是没办法，走吧。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: His remorse is insincere. That gent's relieved to beest able to moveth on my liege.
Zh: 他的懊悔如此虚伪。那位先生可是很欣喜自己又能和阁下一起工作了。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Can't you for just once roll with it, lieutenant?
Zh: “就这么一次都不行吗，警督？”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: I don't think I should talk to him any more.
Zh: “我觉得我和他没什么可谈的了。”

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Oh, of course. I didn't realize that. He looks at the lorries down on the street and turns...
Zh: “哦，当然可以。我只是没想到。”他看着街上的货車，转过身来……

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: Oh, of course. Now it makes sense. He nods, looking down at the lorries.
Zh: “哦，当然可以。现在就说得通了。”他点点头，低头看着那些货車。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
En: That makes absolutely no sense, but I really don't want to get into it with you.
Zh: 这完全说不通，可我实在不想纠结下去了。

ConvTitle: KIM SWITCH / REJOIN AFTER MEASURHEAD TALK
