En: We do. I was able to re-contextualize it as a dark joke. He pauses thoughtfully. "I will not be able to re-contextualize it the next time."
Zh: “要的。根据你的前言后语，我可以把它理解成一个黑色幽默。”他若有所思地停顿了一会儿。“不过下次我就没办法这样理解了。”

En: Yes. Well. I was able to re-contextualize it as a *dark joke*. He pauses thoughtfully. "I will not be able to re-contextualize it the next time."
Zh: “是的。呃。根据你的前言后语，我可以把它理解成一个黑色幽默。”他若有所思地停顿了一会儿。“不过下次我就没办法这样理解了。”

En: I don't know why she left you. Let's go.
Zh: “我不知道她为什么会离开你。我们走吧。”

En: The right words just don't come to him. He tries, but they don't.
Zh: 他就是想不出合适的词来。他试过，但是失败了。

En: The theatrical suicide attempt -- we need to talk about it.
Zh: “这个戏剧性的自杀企图——我们需要聊聊。”

En: I wished to embrace The Gloaming ahead of time. The undoing of the atoms called to me.
Zh: “我想提前拥抱末日黄昏。原子的毁灭在召唤我。”

En: Yes. I want to talk about it as well.
Zh: “好啊。我也想聊聊这个。”

En: Do we have to?
Zh: “一定要聊吗？”

En: It was a cry for help.
Zh: “这是在寻求帮助。”

En: No, we don't. [Leave.]
Zh: “不，不用了。”[离开。]

En: Yes. Well. I was able to re-contextualize it as a dark joke. He pauses thoughtfully. "I will not be able to re-contextualize it the next time."
Zh: “是的。好吧，如果按照你前后的话重新理解的话，我可以把它当成一个黑色幽默。”他若有所思地停顿了一会儿。“不过下次我就没办法这样理解了。”

En: I understand you sometimes employ *extreme* interrogation tactics, but I want you to understand: that was *too* extreme.
Zh: “我能理解，有时候你会使用*极端的*审讯策略，但我想让你明白：那个有点*太过*极端了。”

En: We can't have RCM officers going around, putting guns in their mouths. We can't afford to have that image. Few institutions can.
Zh: “我们不能让RCM警官把枪放在自己的嘴里四处走动。这种场面实在是让人承受不起。几乎没有什么机构可以。”

En: Understood. 100%. It won't happen again.
Zh: “100%理解。我保证再也不会这样了。”

En: I went with my gut feeling and this time it was wrong.
Zh: “我按直觉行事，这次它错了。”

En: It will happen again. Until I succeed. Suicide is painless.
Zh: “这种事还会发生的。直到我成功为止。自杀是没有痛苦的。”

En: It's going to happen again. I'm going to do it again. I'm going try it again *soon*.
Zh: “还会发生这种事的。我还会这么做的，*很快*就会有第二次。”

En: But why did she leave me then?
Zh: “那她为什么会离开我？”

En: I'm sorry I put you into this position.
Zh: “很抱歉让你陷入这种境地。”

En: The Gloaming? He takes off his glasses and cleans them. "Now, that's exactly what we *can't* have. Couldn't have put it better myself. I would need to *re-contextualize* the hell out of that."
Zh: “末日黄昏？”他取下眼镜，轻轻擦拭着。“那正好就是我们*不能*拥有的。我也说不太好了。我要根据你的前言后语*重新理解*一下。”

En: He puts his glasses back on. "Now, let's go. And let's keep guns out of our mouths. And not say anything about any gloaming, or anything like that."
Zh: 他把眼镜戴了回去。“我们走吧。记得别再把枪塞到嘴巴里了。还有，不要说任何末日黄昏，或者其他之类的话。”

En: Good. I'm glad we had this talk. He nods. "After you."
Zh: “很好。很高兴我们进行了这次的谈话。”他点点头。“你先走吧。”

En: Yes. Very, very, *very* wrong. He nods in agreement. "I'm glad you see it my way. Let's go."
Zh: “没错。错的非常，非常，*非常*离谱。”他点头表示赞同。“很高兴你能以我的方式看待这个问题。我们走吧。”

En: No need to apologize -- just keep your gun out of your mouth and we'll be all right. He nods. "Let's move."
Zh: “别必要道歉——只要别再把枪塞进嘴里，我们绝对会没事的。”他点点头。“我们走吧。”

En: No. He takes off his glasses and cleans them. "You're not. You're not going to put your gun into your mouth when we interrogate people. That is never going to happen again."
Zh: “不行。”他取下眼镜，轻轻擦拭着。“不可以。我们在审问别人的时候，你不能把枪放进自己的嘴里。这种事永远不能再次发生。”

En: He puts his glasses back on and repeats: "Never. Now, let's go."
Zh: 他重新戴上眼镜，又重复了一遍：“永远不可以。现在我们走吧。”

En: This was your chance to make amends, but you chose not to. Instead you chose to be the bad-ass suicide boiadeiro and now he doesn't like you.
Zh: 这是你道歉的机会，但你没选择这样做。相反，你选择做一个混账的自杀牛仔，现在他不喜欢你了。

En: Whooh... He takes off his glasses, reaches for a cloth -- pauses -- and puts his glasses back on.
Zh: “呃……”他取下眼镜，掏出一块布——停了一会儿——然后又把眼镜戴了回去。

En: He has an idea who *she* is. Many cops have a *she* or *he* who left. But he just doesn't know what to say.
Zh: 他知道那个*她*是谁了。很多警察都会曾经被一个*她*或者是*他*抛弃过。但他只是不知道说什么好。

En: No, no. We are talking about it. I was able to re-contextualize it as a dark joke. He pauses thoughtfully. "I will not be able to re-contextualize it the next time."
Zh: “不，不行。我们必须要谈谈。根据你的前言后语，我可以把它理解成一个黑色幽默。”他若有所思地停顿了一会儿。“不过下次我就没办法这样理解了。”

En: So. Those voices they heard...
Zh: “那，他们听到的那些声音……”

En: I believe they heard something, he says, glancing back at the twins. "And given the poverty of this part of town, I don't know how they could have heard a radio."
Zh: “他们肯定听到了什么，”他回头看了看那对双胞胎说道。“这个地方这么穷，我不知道他们是怎么听到广播的。”

En: I don't *know* how they heard it, but... He returns his gaze to you. "I know the station they imitated. So they must have heard it somehow."
Zh: “我不*知道*他们是怎么听到的，可是……”他将目光转回你的身上。“我知道他们模仿的是哪个电台。所以他们肯定是听到过。”

En: What channel was it?
Zh: “你说的是哪个频道？”

En: Speedfreaks FM.
Zh: “狂飙怪人.FM。”

En: No hesitation. He knows it well. Probably has the channel saved to his radio.
Zh: 他回答得毫不犹豫，就像是很了解这个电台似的。也许他把这个频道已经存在了他的收音机里。

En: Wait a second. How do you know that station?
Zh: “等等，你是怎么知道那个电台的？”

En: He pauses. "It's... important in our line to know about all sorts of things. I know a little bit about radio culture."
Zh: 他顿了顿。“这个嘛……干我们这行对各种事都得了解一点。我对电台文化也有一些研究。”

En: Speedfreaks FM, huh?
Zh: “狂飙怪人.FM？”

En: Yeah. Local station, known for its kinetic music and aggressive style.
Zh: “对，一个以动感音乐和激进风格而闻名的本地电台。”

En: Sounds like a good time!
Zh: “听起来很不错！”

En: Sounds a bit rough for me. I like tenderness in my radio, I think.
Zh: “听上去不太适合我。我更喜欢听柔和一点的节目。”

En: Sounds like a station for idiots.
Zh: “听起来像是白痴才会听的电台。”

En: Yeah. The lieutenant checks his watch. "Anyway."
Zh: “嗯。”警督看了看手表。“是的吧。”

En: The machinery of his mind is at work, sire! With swiftness the gears do turn, for to produce *deceit*! He seeks to hide his fondness for his favourite channel, Speedfreaks FM!
Zh: 他的大脑在飞速运转，大人！脑筋转那么快，肯定是在编造*谎言*！他想要隐藏自己对狂飙怪人.FM的喜爱之情！

En: Are you a fan of the station, Kim?
Zh: “你喜欢听这个电台吗，金？”

En: Radio culture. Sure.
Zh: “电台文化啊，好吧。”

En: Radio culture, huh? That makes sense.
Zh: “电台文化？那我明白了。”

En: No. And my radio habits are not relevant to this serious murder investigation.
Zh: “并不。再说了，我喜欢听什么广播和调查这起重大谋杀案没什么关系。”

En: Mhm. The lieutenant checks his watch. "Anyway."
Zh: “嗯。”警督看了看手表。“算是吧。”

En: You have seen through the veil he would have drawn o're your eyes. Do with this what thou wilt!
Zh: 你已经看穿了他的谎言，揭开了那蒙蔽你双眼的面纱。做你想做的事情吧！

En: The lieutenant isn't usually a man who checks his watch without reason. He was trying to stay cool. Don't push now -- keep an eye out for it again later.
Zh: 警督不会没有缘由地看表。他只是想保持冷静。别着急——再等等看。

En: Yes, of course! Earlier, when you turned the radio on in his Kineema!
Zh: 当然了！之前你在他的锐影里打开收音机的时候就该发现了！

En: Don't.
Zh: 别这样。

En: Speedfreaks FM! Your favourite channel!
Zh: “狂飙怪人.FM！就是你最爱的频道！”

En: Wasn't your radio tuned into Speedfreaks FM?
Zh: “你的收音机不是调到了狂飙怪人.FM吗？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: The lieutenant breathes steadily, each inhalation and exhalation controlled.
Zh: 警督平稳地控制着自己的每一次呼吸。

En: You must be mistaken, the lieutenant says in a calm, measured tone. "Anyway."
Zh: “你一定是弄错了，”警督用沉稳慎重的语气说道。“随便吧。”

En: A calculated response. To say *why the hell did you snoop on my radio?* would be reasonable -- but it would confirm he likes the station. Instead, he dismisses it. He must be embarrassed by his affection for Speedfreaks FM.
Zh: 这是他思考之后的回答。要是他说*你他妈为什么要偷听我的收音机？*反而更合理——但这样会坐实了他喜欢这个电台。所以他选择不去理会。他对狂飙怪人.FM的喜爱一定让他很尴尬。

En: I can't explain how they heard it, the lieutenant continues unabated. "I believe they heard something. And given the poverty of this part of town, I don't know how they could have heard a radio. However..."
Zh: “我说不清他们是怎么听到的，”警督继续说道。“总之肯定是听到了什么。可是这个地区这么穷，我不知道他们是怎么听上广播的。可是……”

En: Weird as it is, this situation is not connected to the murder. This isn't our business.
Zh: “确实奇怪，可这跟谋杀案没有联系。这不是我们应该管的事。”

En: He was clearly uncomfortable discussing it before. He still is. You have nothing to gain from pushing his buttons.
Zh: 很显然，他一直都想回避这个问题。惹怒他对你没有任何好处。

En: I can't explain how they heard it, he says, ignoring your comment. "I believe they heard something. And given the poverty of this part of town, I don't know how they could have heard a radio. However..."
Zh: “我说不清他们是怎么听到的，”他没有理会你，继续说道。“总之肯定是听到了什么。可是这个地区这么贫穷，我不知道他们是怎么听上广播的。可是……”

En: Maybe it is connected, though. Ruby had all those frequencies in her lorry, remember?
Zh: “也许这两件事有关联。露比的货車里也有那些频率，你记得吗？”

En: Are you just embarrassed that we may have to contact Speedfreaks FM?
Zh: “你是因为我们要联络狂飙怪人.FM所以觉得尴尬吗？”

En: It's a mystery though. Mysteries are our business.
Zh: “这件事的确是个谜。但是解开谜题是我们的工作。”

En: But it is related, Kim. The wind told me so.
Zh: “可这两件事就是有关联，金。这是风告诉我的 。”

En: The lieutenant raises an eyebrow. "Well. That is true..."
Zh: 警督挑起了眉毛。“好吧，的确……”

En: No. His answer is immediate. "I just... don't see how this furthers our investigation into a murder. That's all."
Zh: “不。”他立刻答道。“我只是……觉得这对谋杀案的调查没有任何帮助。仅此而已。”

En: An ocean breeze blows in strong. He raises his jacket collar against it.
Zh: 海风吹得更猛烈了。他拉高夹克的衣领想要挡住风。

En: (Cough under your breath.) "Seolite spy-tech."
Zh: （低声咳嗽。）“西奥人的间谍技术。”

En: Entroponetic crosstalk?
Zh: “灰域干扰？”

En: If it was... that can't be it. The lieutenant frowns.
Zh: 警督蹙眉。“如果是这样……那是不可能的。”

En: Maybe they're psychic recruit material for the Remote Viewers Division?
Zh: “也许他们是遥视者部队的通灵招募对象？”

En: Even if it was a radio, where was it coming from?
Zh: “就算有收音机，那又是从哪儿来的？”

En: I got nothing.
Zh: “我什么都不知道。”

En: Let's at least rule out radios before continuing. Maybe the twins didn't know it was a radio they were hearing...
Zh: 他又摇了摇头。“在继续之前，先把收音机的问题解决了吧。也许这对双胞胎并不知道他们听的是电台……”

En: No. You're not going to try to recruit the twins to the Remote Viewers Division...
Zh: “不会，谁都不会招募这对双胞胎进遥视者部队……”

En: The lieutenant shrugs.
Zh: 警督耸耸肩。

En: Neither do I. He sighs.
Zh: “我也不会。”他叹了口气。

En: Didn't Ruby have advanced radio know-how?
Zh: “露比不是很有无线电技术吗？”

En: Maybe it's related to what happened in the church -- Soona's experiment...
Zh: “也许和教堂发生的事情有关——苏娜的实验……”

En: The lieutenant is grimly silent.
Zh: 警督沉默不语，面容可怖。

En: We're going to stay focused on the *case*, officer, the lieutenant says, words heavy with anger.
Zh: “我们要把精力放在*案子*上，警官，”警督愤怒地说道。

En: Maybe it has something to do with the mass call-in *radio* game?
Zh: “也许和大型来电直播的*电台*游戏有关？”

En: That doesn't seem likely to me.
Zh: “我觉得不大可能。”

En: The washerwoman seems to know everything that happens here. Let's ask if she heard anything, before moving on.
Zh: “那个洗衣女工好像很了解这里的事情。在继续调查之前，先去问问她有没有听到过什么吧。”

En: Yes. Mysteries like *who killed the hanged man* are our business. Not this...
Zh: “没错。我们的工作是解开*是谁杀了那个吊人*这种谜题。而不是这种事……”

En: The lieutenant says nothing. An ocean breeze blows in strong. He raises his jacket collar against it.
Zh: 警督一言不发。海风吹得更猛烈了。他拉高夹克的衣领想要挡住风。

En: After you. The lieutenant gestures toward the old woman.
Zh: 警督指了指那个老妇人。“你先请。”

En: I suspect you're embarrassed to call Speedfreaks FM, Kim.
Zh: “我怀疑你不好意思和狂飙怪人.FM联系，金。”

En: Besides. Maybe it was just a radio they overheard -- they might not be old enough to know the difference. How would they have heard the voices?
Zh: “再说了。或许他们只是无意间听到了电台——他们年纪还小，听不出区别。他们怎么会听到那些声音呢？”

En: A warm smile.
Zh: 一个温暖的笑容。

En: That's why his clothes fit so well...
Zh: 这就是他的衣服如此合身的原因……

En: My *style*? What about it?
Zh: “我的*风格*？怎么了？”

En: The lieutenant's right. It gives you all the *panache* of a bicycle courier, daredevil and commander of the city streets!
Zh: 警督是对的。它给了你一个自行車信使应有的*派头*，让你能变成整条城市街道上最鲁莽大胆的指挥官！

En: Anyway, we should probably get back to the case. Let's go.
Zh: “无所谓了，我们应该回到案子上来。走吧。”

En: For starters, you're wearing *the exact same* outfit I first met you in... He holds his nose.
Zh: “首先，从我第一次见到你的时候，你一直穿着*完全一样*的衣服……”他捏了捏鼻子。

En: It's disco, Kim.
Zh: “这是迪斯科风，金。”

En: Yeah, maybe I should let these threads air out a little bit.
Zh: “是啊，也许我应该让这些线团透透气。”

En: I'm glad you've finally started dressing a little more like an officer of the RCM. Keep it up.
Zh: “我很高兴，你总算穿的更像是RCM的警官了。继续保持。”

En: You have developed a bit of a *rind* over the past few days.
Zh: 最近这几天，你已经长出了一些*新皮*。

En: The lieutenant's right. You're the acrid sweet smell of decay that makes people evacuate the room.
Zh: 警督是对的。你是人们撤离房间时留下的那股腐烂的辛甜味。”

En: Somehow you thought you were *concealing* it, but now you know that *everybody* has noticed.
Zh: 不知道为什么，你觉得自己在*隐藏*它，但是现在你知道*所有人*都注意到了。

En: Seeing you on the verge of tears, the lieutenant realizes he was maybe overly direct vis-a-vis your sartorial situation...
Zh: 看到快要哭出来的你，警督意识到他对你的着装问题表达地可能有些过于直接了……

En: Now, now, no need to despair, detective. You know the expression 'The clothes make the man'? We just need to find you a *new style*. That jacket you just acquired is a decent start.
Zh: “现在，没必要绝望，警探。你知道有句话叫‘人靠衣装’吗？我们只需要帮你找到一种*新风格*就好了。你刚弄到的那件夹克就是个很好的开始。”

En: Have you even *smelled* that jacket? It's like some wretched night-wandering creature died in it.
Zh: “你到底有没有*闻过*那件夹克？感觉好像有个可怜的夜间动物死在了里面一样。”

En: It's good to see you looking like a proud officer of the RCM. It's important for the people of Martinaise to know we're here.
Zh: “很高兴见到你穿的像个值得骄傲的RCM警官了。让马丁内斯的人知道我们的存在是很重要的。”

En: Regular cop, regular clothes.
Zh: “普通的警察，普通的衣服。”

En: You *like* this? I feel like it's really cramping my style.
Zh: “你*喜欢*这个吗？我觉得它真的束缚了我的风格。”

En: Say, why aren't *you* wearing a pig suit?
Zh: “话说，*你*为什么没穿警服？”

En: It's, well, a little *eclectic*, don't you think? How would you even describe what you're wearing?
Zh: “它是，呃，有点*不拘一格*，你不觉得吗？你该怎么描述自己现在这身打扮？”

En: The lieutenant regards you for a moment, unsure whether you're making a joke or not, and if so, whether he should acknowledge it...
Zh: 警督注视了你一会儿，不确定你是不是真的在开玩笑，如果真是这样的话，他是否应该承认……

En: Right, detective. You are one regular cop.
Zh: “没错，警探。你是个普通的警察。”

En: You're an officer of the RCM, not some *bon vivant*...
Zh: “你是RCM的一名警官，不是什么*享乐主义者*……”

En: You know the expression 'The clothes make the man'? The right outfit, in the right situation, can make all the difference in the world.
Zh: “你知道有句话叫‘人靠衣装’吗？在适当的场景选择正确的着装，能让这个世界的一切都变得截然不同。”

En: If by 'disco' you mean 'ungainly and sad,' then yes, it's *extremely* disco.
Zh: “如果你说的‘迪斯科’是指‘笨拙和悲伤’，那就是了，它*极其*迪斯科。”

En: It would be better if you let them air out in the trash, where they belong...
Zh: “如果你把它们放进垃圾堆里透气就更好了，那才是属于它们的地方……”

En: For starters, you're dressed like an old fascist. Is that really appropriate?
Zh: “首先，你穿的像个老法西斯主义分子。这样真的合适吗？”

En: I don't care about their politics, it just *looks* cool.
Zh: “我不在乎什么政治，只是*看起来*很酷罢了。”

En: Well, all that FALN gear makes you look like a bicycle courier. All you're missing is the shirt.
Zh: “好吧，那套FALN装备让你看起来像个自行車信使。唯一缺的就只有那件衬衫了。”

En: To be perfectly blunt, you're dressed like a blood-hungry mercenary.
Zh: “坦率的讲，你穿的就像个嗜血的雇佣兵。”

En: That's not quite what I was going for.
Zh: “那不是我想要的。”

En: Yes. And this 'mega-binoclard' knows how to sew a lapel and center a back vent. The lieutenant raises one eyebrow at you.
Zh: “是的。而且这个‘四眼仔’知道要翻领和调整背衩。”警督扬起一侧的眉毛。

En: I'm not taking 'style' tips from someone who dresses like a mega-binoclard.
Zh: “我才不会从一个穿的像四眼仔的人那里接受什么‘着装风格’建议。”

En: So if you want to wake up next to anyone other than *Commodore Red*, you might do well to take notes.
Zh: “如果你希望自己醒来的时候，身旁除了*海军准将红朗姆*之外谁也没有，那你可能要做好笔记。”

En: Then maybe you should change your look...
Zh: “也许你应该换一身打扮……”

En: The lieutenant sighs. "It might feel good to play *super-merc*, but you might want to ask if it's actually helping you solve the case..."
Zh: 警督叹了一口气。“玩*超级雇佣兵*游戏可能感觉挺好的，但是你也许应该问问自己，这样是不是真能帮你解决这起案件……”

En: What happens when I find the shirt?
Zh: “如果我找到衬衫，会怎么样？”

En: Who knows? The lieutenant shrugs. "Perhaps something *miraculous*."
Zh: “谁知道？”警督耸耸肩。“也许会发生某种*奇迹*吧。”

En: Really? You think so?
Zh: “真的吗？你真这么觉得？”

En: Officers above the rank of sergeant are granted wide discretion when it comes to their uniforms...
Zh: “拥有警长以上头衔的警官选择制服的自由度会大一些……”

En: The idea is that civilians are more likely to open up to someone who's not dressed like a typical cop...
Zh: “问题是平民更愿意向那些穿得不像典型警察的人敞开心扉……”

En: The lieutenant sighs. "Is that really the impression you want to give people?"
Zh: 警督叹了口气。“你真想给别人留下这种印象吗？”

En: Okay. (Nod.) "You're a sharp dressed man. We could be style buddies."
Zh: “好吧。”（点头。）“你是个穿着很有品味的男人。跟你一起我也可以变得有范儿一点。”

En: I'm wearing it in case we get into a firefight.
Zh: “我穿这个是为了防止跟别人交战。”

En: That's right. You're protected in your ceramic carapace. Whatever harm you inflict on that world won't be visited on you.
Zh: 没错。你被保护在你的陶瓷甲壳里。不论世界上有何种何样的伤害，都不会降临到你的头上。

En: You're wearing the vestments of *order* and *hierarchy*. You have nothing to answer for.
Zh: 你穿着*秩序*与*层级*的法衣。你不需要对任何东西负责。

En: Except all the war crimes the people in those vestments perpetrated, but what's a few atrocities here and there if you *look cool* doing it?
Zh: 只是那些穿法衣的人犯下了所有战争罪行，但是如果穿起来*看着很酷*的话，稍微来那么一点小暴行又有什么的呢？

En: Your half-brother is proud to stand beside you.
Zh: 你那不是血亲但胜似血亲的兄弟很骄傲地站在你的身边。

En: 'Eclectic' is for pop music with 'indigenous' percussion. *You're* a sartorial maverick.
Zh: ‘不拘一格’是为使用‘本土’打击乐器的流行音乐准备的。而*你*是一个在着装上特立独行的人。

En: They're just clothes, Kim. Who cares?
Zh: “它们只是衣服，金。谁在乎呢？”

En: To be perfectly blunt, you're dressed like a bum. And you smell like one, too.
Zh: “坦率的讲，你穿的就像个流浪汉。而且身上的味道也像。”

En: Then maybe you should change your look...
Zh: “也许你应该换一身打扮……”

En: That's not quite what I was going for.
Zh: “那不是我想要的。”

En: That's right -- You're an entrepreneur of the peace, here to disrupt legacy law enforcement with buzzwords and privatization schemes.
Zh: 没有错——你是和平的企业家，来这儿是为了用流行词汇和私有化草案破坏遗产法案的执行。

En: It's just, well, you look more like a high-flying businessman than a police officer.
Zh: “只是，呃，你看起来更像是个野心勃勃的商人，而不是一个警察。”

En: You have developed a bit of a *rind* over the past few days.
Zh: 最近这几天，你已经长出了一些*新皮*。

En: The lieutenant's right. You're the acrid sweet smell of decay that makes respectable people wrinkle their noses, the room-evacuating fart...
Zh: 警督是对的。你是能让那些可敬的人皱起鼻子的腐烂辛甜味，是能把所有人轰出屋子的臭屁……

En: Somehow you thought you were *concealing* it, but now you know that *everybody* has noticed.
Zh: 不知道为什么，你觉得自己在*隐藏*它，但是现在你知道*所有人*都注意到了。

En: Seeing you on the verge of tears, the lieutenant realizes he was maybe overly direct vis-a-vis your sartorial situation...
Zh: 看到快要哭出来的你，警督意识到他对你的着装问题表达地可能有些过于直接了……

En: I'm the hobocop, Kim, what do you expect?
Zh: “我是流浪警察，金，你在期待什么呢？”

En: But the gloves keep my hands warm -- look. (Show him the gloves.)
Zh: “但是手套能温暖我的手——看。”（给他看看手套。）

En: The lieutenant sighs.
Zh: 警督叹了口气。

En: That's not quite what I was going for.
Zh: “那不是我想要的。”

En: Then maybe you should change your look...
Zh: “也许你应该换一身打扮……”

En: Let's not get ahead of ourselves, detective...
Zh: “我们还是不要想太多了，警探……”

En: Office Harbinger.
Zh: “先驱警官。”

En: Regular-core?
Zh: “普通核心？”

En: Superstar Casual. (Click your tongue.)
Zh: “超级巨星休闲风。”（咂咂舌头。）

En: Hobo chic.
Zh: “优雅流浪汉。”

En: Formal apologetic.
Zh: “正式道歉风。”

En: Well, I can't say it doesn't *fit* you...
Zh: “呃，也不能说它*不适合*你……”

En: Still, you might try *branching out* a little. You know the expression 'The clothes make the man'? The right outfit, in the right situation, can make all the difference in the world.
Zh: “不过，你还是应该试着*扩展一下范围*。你知道有句话叫‘人靠衣装’吗？在适当的场景选择正确的着装，能让这个世界的一切都变得截然不同。”

En: That's not quite what I was going for.
Zh: “那不是我想要的。”

En: Then maybe you should change your look...
Zh: “也许你应该换一身打扮……”

En: Well, yeah, I want everyone to know I'm here to defend traditional values and stomp communards.
Zh: “嗯，是啊，我想让所有人知道我是来守护传统价值观、践踏公社社员的。”

En: Then maybe you should change your look...
Zh: “也许你应该换一身打扮……”

En: That's not quite what I was going for.
Zh: “那不是我想要的。”

En: Well, yeah. You never know when you're going to need to pitch a business proposal.
Zh: “呃，是啊。你永远也不知道什么时候就需要推销一份商业计划书了。”

En: Oh, I just thought that this was how professional people were supposed to dress.
Zh: “哦，我还以为专业人士就应该这么穿。”

En: Oh, god. Don't start *talking* like those people, too...
Zh: “哦，天啦。那可千万别学那些人一样*说话*……”

En: There's nothing *wrong* with it, per se. It's just... a little much...
Zh: “本身没什么*问题*。只是有点……太过了……”

En: I'm not taking 'style' tips from someone who dresses like a washed-up TipTop racer.
Zh: “我才不会从一个穿的像个*过气大师赛車手*的人那里接受什么‘着装风格’建议。”

En: This 'washed-up TipTop racer' knows how to sew a lapel and center a back vent. The lieutenant raises one eyebrow at you.
Zh: “这位*过气大师赛車手*知道要翻领和调整背衩。”警督扬起一侧的眉毛。
